target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output — the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  → OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  → session write lock released during retry window
  → incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  → activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  → transcript changes without updating fence
  → original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  → EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  → model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  → outer layer hits generic failure branch
  → assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  → message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Seven coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend. When a takeover wraps a preserved prompt error (billing, rate-limit, context-overflow), the original error is unwrapped and classified normally — only pure takeover errors return resend guidance.

### 3. Scope SDK retry disable to embedded prompt lock path (`attempt.ts`, `attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Extracted `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` helper that wraps the session's `streamFn` with `{ maxRetries: 0, ...options }` — the `0` is a **default** that goes before the caller options spread, so `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` (surfaced via `options.maxRetries` per the chain in `sdk.ts:360`) takes precedence when explicitly configured. Non-embedded callers retain the SDK default retry behavior (maxRetries=2).

### 4. Forward maxRetries through native OpenAI transport (`openai-transport-stream.ts`)
`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` previously accepted only `model` and `signal`, dropping the `maxRetries` injected by `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault`. Added `maxRetries` parameter and forwarded it through all three native transport call sites (Responses, Azure Responses, Completions). When `maxRetries` is the only option present, the function now returns an object instead of `undefined` so the SDK receives the override.

### 5. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible — the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

### 6. Restore unconfigured connection-error retry at the orchestrator layer (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts`)
Disabling SDK retries inside the prompt-lock release window (#3) is required so an in-window retry cannot widen the takeover race — but for users who have **not** configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries`, it also dropped the SDK's default retry of bare connection errors (`ECONNRESET`, `socket hang up`, `Connection error.`) that carry no leading HTTP status. This is restored at the orchestrator layer, where the existing one-shot `consumeTransientHttpRetry()` re-runs the full primary→fallback cycle and **re-acquires the session lock per attempt**, so it does not widen any single release window. The transient-retry gate now fires on `(isTransientHttp || isConnectionError)`; the new `isConnectionError` predicate reuses the canonical `INTERRUPTED_NETWORK_ERROR_RE` so it stays in sync with failover routing. In-window `maxRetries:0` is unchanged. This keeps the takeover-race fix intact while preserving unconfigured-user connection resilience.

### 7. Restore unconfigured request-timeout retry at the orchestrator layer (`agent-runner-execution.ts`)
The orchestrator transient-retry restore in #6 left a symmetric gap: in addition to bare connection errors, the OpenAI/Anthropic SDKs retry request-**timeout** transport errors by default, and after the in-window `maxRetries:0`, an unconfigured single-model user's timeout error is rethrown to the same outer gate — which only matched HTTP-status transients and connection errors, dropping timeout retry entirely (multi-model users were unaffected because failover already routes timeouts). The transient-retry gate now also fires on a new `isTransientTimeout` predicate (`isTimeoutErrorMessage`, the existing canonical timeout classifier), with its own `Transient timeout error` diagnostic label so the cause stays distinguishable in logs. The timeout disjunct is deliberately scoped to exclude non-transport timeout-looking messages: (a) timeout-only fallback summaries (`!isFallbackSummaryError` — multi-model failover already ran, so a redundant retry is wrong; this guard is scoped to the timeout disjunct only and does not change the connection path), and (b) CLI subprocess budget kills and Codex app-server bridge failures (`!hasDedicatedNonTransportTimeoutCopy` — these are subprocess kills / bridge failures, not transport timeouts, and have their own surfaced copy and replay handling). The one-shot `consumeTransientHttpRetry()` budget is shared, so timeout retry is bounded exactly like the connection retry. The connection-retry path semantics from #6 are unchanged. The timeout disjunct additionally excludes local non-provider runtime coordination errors (e.g. `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError`, whose message reads as `session file locked (timeout ...ms)`) via the canonical `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classifier: retrying any model would hit the same local condition, so these abort the fallback chain rather than re-run as a transport timeout.

## Real behavior proof

> **Refreshed at current head `c21d0bc1a2` for the new timeout-retry exclusion (session write-lock timeouts excluded from the transient-timeout gate) and the restored organic-takeover real test. Supersedes the earlier blocks below.**

- **Behavior addressed**: The transient-timeout retry restore (Fix #7) used `isTimeoutErrorMessage`, which also matches local `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` messages (`session file locked (timeout ...ms): ...`). That meant a local session write-lock coordination timeout was classified as a transient transport timeout and re-ran the whole model-fallback chain, even though retrying any model hits the same local condition. The timeout disjunct now excludes non-provider runtime coordination errors via the canonical `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classifier — the same seam `model-fallback.ts` already uses to abort the chain on coordination errors. The connection/HTTP disjuncts are unchanged.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `c21d0bc1a2`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, deps installed with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
- **Exact steps run after this patch**: Ran the affected suites at this head with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <suite>`: the new session-write-lock-timeout exclusion regression in `agent-runner-execution.test.ts`, and the organic real-setup session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence). The real-setup test depends on the `embedded-agent-helpers` mock exporting `isConnectionError`/`isTimeoutErrorMessage` (called eagerly on the catch path); those exports were missing, so this run also restores them so the test imports and runs to its assertion.
- **Observed result**: a session-write-lock-shaped timeout error now terminates as `run_failed` and is NOT re-run through the fallback chain (`runWithModelFallback` called exactly once); the organic takeover still surfaces cause-neutral resend guidance. The exclusion is proven non-tautological: removing the `!isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError(err)` clause makes the regression fail with the mock called twice.
- **Evidence after fix** (current-head terminal output):
```
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (current head c21d0bc1a2) ===
Environment: Darwin arm64, Node v24.10.0
Commit: c21d0bc1a2c8cf7bb535dafb00299b33f9857498

--- A: Session write-lock timeout must NOT consume a transient-timeout retry (regression for the new exclusion) ---
 ✓  auto-reply  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts > runAgentTurnWithFallback > does not retry a session write-lock timeout through the transient timeout gate (#87180)
   asserts runWithModelFallback called exactly once + terminal run_failed (NOT re-run through the fallback chain)
   (proven non-tautological: removing the !isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError(err) clause makes this test fail with "called 2 times")
 Test Files  1 passed (1) ; Tests  228 passed (228)

--- B: Organic real-setup session-takeover proof (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence) ---
 ✓  auto-reply  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError  316ms
   (this real test now imports and runs to its assertion after restoring the missing isConnectionError/isTimeoutErrorMessage mock exports it depends on)
 Test Files  1 passed (1) ; Tests  1 passed (1)

--- C: Code-path verification at current head ---
Timeout disjunct now excludes local non-provider runtime coordination errors (e.g. session write-lock timeouts):
3232:        !isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError(err);
=== PROOF COMPLETE @ c21d0bc1a2 ===
```
- **What was not tested**: The exact live Feishu/DeepSeek production timing race is not replayed end-to-end; the strongest proof remains the deterministic local HTTP + on-disk session takeover coverage plus the session-write-lock-timeout exclusion regression above.

### Earlier proof (head `e0ec982bb8`)


- **Behavior addressed**: For users who have not configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries`, the in-window `maxRetries: 0` (required so an in-window SDK retry cannot widen the takeover race) previously also dropped the SDK's default retry of bare connection errors (ECONNRESET, socket hang up, `Connection error.`). That resilience is restored at the orchestrator layer, where each retry re-acquires the session lock and does not widen any single release window. The in-window `maxRetries: 0` race fix is unchanged.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0dc5a3010a`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, deps installed with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
- **Exact steps run after this patch**: Ran the affected suites at this head with `node_modules/.bin/vitest run <suite>`: the orchestrator connection-error retry cases (one-retry-then-success and bounded-loop), the HTTP transient 521 diagnostic case (the previously-red shard, now green with the preserved wording), the `isConnectionError` predicate unit coverage, the organic real-setup session-takeover regression (real on-disk session + real local HTTP model server + real OpenAI-completions transport + real prompt-lock fence), and the in-window session-lock suite; plus `tsgo:core`/`tsgo:core:test` typecheck and oxlint on the changed files.
- **Observed result**: connection errors now trigger exactly one full-cycle orchestrator retry (lock re-acquired per attempt) then succeed or terminate without looping; the HTTP diagnostic wording is preserved; the organic takeover still surfaces cause-neutral resend guidance; in-window `maxRetries: 0` is intact.
- **Evidence after fix** (current-head terminal output):
```
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (current head 0dc5a3010a) ===
Environment: Darwin arm64, Node v24.10.0
Commit: 0dc5a3010af99cbbea1e0ea81851c6a756048bf2

--- A: Orchestrator-layer connection-error retry restoration (named cases) ---
 ✓  auto-reply  ../../src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts > runAgentTurnWithFallback > retries the full fallback cycle once for a bare connection error, then succeeds (#87180 compat) 98ms
 ✓  auto-reply  ../../src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts > runAgentTurnWithFallback > stops after a single connection-error retry instead of looping when it keeps failing 2ms

--- B: HTTP transient diagnostic wording preserved (the prior red shard) ---
 ✓  auto-reply  ../../src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts > runReplyAgent transient HTTP retry > retries once after transient 521 HTML failure and then succeeds 3856ms

--- C: isConnectionError predicate direct coverage ---
 ✓  agents-support  ../../src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.test.ts > isConnectionError > does not treat "invalid_api_key: the provided credential is incorrect" as a connection error 0ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  28 passed | 10 skipped (38)
   Duration  405ms (transform 183ms, setup 124ms, import 137ms, tests 33ms, environment 0ms)

--- D: Organic real-setup session-takeover proof (resend guidance, cause-neutral) ---
 ✓  auto-reply  ../../src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > runAgentTurnWithFallback — real session takeover (#87180) > surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 338ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
```

### Real session-takeover path (organic error, real transport + real fence)

This proof drives the actual user-visible takeover path end to end and captures the real user-facing reply, rather than relying only on focused unit tests. Committed as `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`.

**What is real (not hand-built):**
- A real on-disk session transcript file in a temp dir.
- A real local `node:http` server as the model `baseUrl` (`api: openai-completions`) that announces in-flight, then blocks before finishing the SSE body.
- The real `openai-completions` transport doing real HTTP I/O.
- The real embedded prompt-lock controller + fence (`createEmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`), wired through the real `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` / `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` helpers — the exact helpers `attempt.ts` uses in production.
- The `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` is thrown **organically** by `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")` when a concurrent steering write changes the session-file fingerprint during the released-lock window — it is **not** constructed by hand.
- The real `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classification and the real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch branch producing the user-facing payload.

**Mechanism (deterministic Promise handshakes, no sleeps):**
1. A real temp session file is created with one session line.
2. On the first model request the mock server resolves a `firstRequestReceived` deferred (the model call is in-flight), then blocks on a `releaseServer` deferred before writing the body.
3. `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` calls `releaseForPrompt()` at the start of the model call (opening the takeover window) and drains the real transport stream, so the released-lock window spans the real HTTP block.
4. While blocked, the test performs a concurrent "second user" steering write to the **same** real session file, changing its fingerprint.
5. `releaseServer` resolves; the server finishes the SSE body; the wrapped `finally{}` calls `reacquireAfterPrompt()` → `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")`.
6. The fingerprint mismatch trips the fence → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` thrown organically → real classification → real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch.

**Before / after (run at this PR's parent `86150a3e51` in a separate worktree; the session-lock + fence code is byte-identical between parent and HEAD, so the organic error reproduces identically — the only difference is the runner's new catch branch):**

Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. target.title: fix(reply): keep configured ACP bindings from dropping the first turn
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #97186.

Configured `type: "acp"` bindings could finish session initialization but still lose the first inbound turn when no `reply_dispatch` hook claimed the ACP session. The result matched the report: session created, backend process spawned, but no `systemSent`, no transcript, and no visible reply.

## What Problem This Solves

Telegram-style ACP sessions can be routed to a configured ACP target session before any reply-dispatch hook claims the event. When that hook path declines the event, the first user turn is silently dropped instead of being dispatched to the ACP runtime, so the conversation never produces a visible reply.

## What changed

- Build the ACP `reply_dispatch` event once and reuse it.
- If the hook path does not handle an ACP-bound session, fall back to calling the core ACP dispatch runtime directly before embedded-model fallback.
- Add a regression test that uses a configured Telegram-style ACP session key and proves the first turn still reaches `runTurn()` even when the hook declines to claim it.

## Why this fixes the bug

Configured ACP bindings already rewrite the inbound session key to the ACP target session. The missing piece was a core fallback for that target when hook wiring was absent or declined the event. With this change, ACP-bound sessions no longer depend on the hook path to process the first prompt.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts`
- `pnpm_config_verify_deps_before_run=false pnpm build`

target.title: feat(tools/write): add append mode for agent writes
target.body:
## Summary

Revives and rebases the closed append-mode work from #75549 for the canonical data-loss issue #40001.

The `write` tool currently overwrites existing files. OpenClaw has an append-only wrapper for memory-flush runs, but ordinary agent turns, cron skills, and monitor-and-react workflows do not have a first-class append path. This PR adds a backwards-compatible optional `append: true` parameter to `write` so agents can append without synthesizing a read-concatenate-overwrite sequence.

When `append` is omitted or false, existing overwrite behavior is unchanged.

## What changed

- Adds optional `append?: boolean` handling for host/workspace and sandbox write operations.
- Preserves existing workspace/root guard behavior.
- Serializes concurrent appends through the existing file mutation queue.
- Preserves validation behavior for blank paths and invalid content.
- Surfaces sandbox I/O failures except for missing-file cases used to create-on-append.
- Honors aborted append calls before entering the mutation queue.
- Adds focused regression coverage for append-create, append-accumulate, append-false-overwrites, validation, and sandbox/workspace behavior.

## Validation

Run in an isolated checkout on current `origin/main`:

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.read.host-tilde-expansion.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.params.test.ts`
  - passed: `10 files / 170 tests`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm lint:core`
  - passed: `0 warnings / 0 errors`

## Notes

This PR intentionally keeps the API small and backwards-compatible. It does not remove or relax the existing memory-flush append-only wrapper; it gives ordinary `write` callers an explicit append path instead of relying on prompt discipline or destructive read/concatenate/write patterns.

Fixes #40001.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** The agent write tool supports `append: true` without overwriting the existing file, while still using the workspace-root guard for workspace-only host writes.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux using the actual `createHostWorkspaceWriteTool` implementation against a temporary workspace on the real filesystem.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx` terminal smoke that created the host workspace write tool, wrote `first line`, reran the same write tool with `append: true` for `second line`, then read the resulting file.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the smoke:

  ```text
  workspace: $TMPDIR/openclaw-write-append-proof-.../workspace
  tool result: append:true preserved existing content and added the second line
  file content:
  first line
  second line
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The final file retained the original content and appended the new content in order; the append path did not truncate or replace the file.
- **What was not tested:** No live Gateway session was exercised; validation used the real tool implementation in an isolated local checkout plus targeted regression/type checks.

target.title: test(firecrawl): add unit tests for firecrawl client functions
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The `firecrawl-client.ts` module provides URL safety validation, search-item extraction, and scrape payload parsing for the Firecrawl web tools plugin. However, this module lacked unit tests to verify the client behavior, which could lead to regressions when the functions are modified.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add unit tests for `assertFirecrawlScrapeTargetAllowed`, `resolveSearchItems`, and `parseFirecrawlScrapePayload` to verify client behavior including URL validation, multi-shape search response extraction, scrape payload parsing, and truncation boundaries. This improves test coverage and prevents regressions.

The module already exported these functions through its `testing` object, confirming they were intended for direct unit coverage.

Focused tests cover:
- **assertFirecrawlScrapeTargetAllowed**: valid public URLs, invalid strings, non-HTTP protocols, private/loopback IPv4/IPv6, blocked hostnames, embedded credentials, missing scheme
- **resolveSearchItems**: all 6 candidate-array paths, empty/missing payloads, non-object entries, URL/title/description/content/published field fallbacks, metadata precedence, empty-string edge cases
- **parseFirecrawlScrapePayload**: markdown/content fallback, no-content throws, text vs markdown mode, metadata enrichment, truncation boundaries (exact, 1-over, zero), warning field handling, type safety

## User Impact

No user-visible change. The Firecrawl client helpers now have comprehensive unit coverage, reducing the risk of regressions when the functions are modified.

## Evidence

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds direct coverage for existing helper behavior rather than reporting a user-visible runtime bug. Source inspection confirms the helper behavior the tests target.

Direct behavior probe:

```text
$ node --import tsx -e "import('./extensions/firecrawl/src/firecrawl-client.js').then((m) => { const t = m.testing; const results = []; try { t.assertFirecrawlScrapeTargetAllowed('https://example.com'); results.push({desc:'valid HTTPS', pass: true}); } catch(e) { results.push({desc:'valid HTTPS', pass: false, err: e.message}); } try { t.assertFirecrawlScrapeTargetAllowed('ftp://bad.com'); results.push({desc:'blocked FTP', pass: false}); } catch(e) { results.push({desc:'blocked FTP', pass: true}); } try { t.assertFirecrawlScrapeTargetAllowed('http://127.0.0.1'); results.push({desc:'blocked localhost', pass: false}); } catch(e) { results.push({desc:'blocked localhost', pass: true}); } const items = t.resolveSearchItems({ data: [{ url:'https://a.com', title:'A' }, { url:'https://b.com', title:'B' }] }); results.push({desc:'resolveSearchItems count', pass: items.length === 2}); const parsed = t.parseFirecrawlScrapePayload({ url: 'https://example.com/page', extractMode: 'markdown', maxChars: 50000, payload: { data: { markdown: '# Hello' } } }); results.push({desc:'parseFirecrawlScrapePayload extractor', pass: parsed.extractor === 'firecrawl'}); results.push({desc:'parseFirecrawlScrapePayload has text', pass: typeof parsed.text === 'string' && parsed.text.length > 0}); console.log(JSON.stringify(results, null, 2)); });
[
  {
    "desc": "valid HTTPS",
    "pass": true
  },
  {
    "desc": "blocked FTP",
    "pass": true
  },
  {
    "desc": "blocked localhost",
    "pass": true
  },
  {
    "desc": "resolveSearchItems count",
    "pass": true
  },
  {
    "desc": "parseFirecrawlScrapePayload extractor",
    "pass": true
  },
  {
    "desc": "parseFirecrawlScrapePayload has text",
    "pass": true
  }
]
```

Unit test run:

```text
$ pnpm test extensions/firecrawl/src/firecrawl-client.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  52 passed (52)
```

target.title: fix(agents): add tool-activity heartbeat to keep subagent alive during tool calls
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Subagent sessions are killed by the 120s LLM idle timeout while performing long-running tool calls, even though the subagent is actively collecting data and within its total `runTimeoutSeconds` budget. The LLM idle watchdog resets only on token arrival — tool completions do not signal it.

## Summary

- **Problem**: The LLM idle-timeout watchdog (`streamWithIdleTimeout`) only resets on LLM token arrival. During subagent tool calls (web_fetch, read, exec, etc.), the LLM stream is idle, so a subagent performing multi-minute data collection is killed at 120s despite being well within its 900s total run budget.
- **Solution**: Implement a per-run tool-activity heartbeat:
  1. `notifyToolActivity(runId)` — published from embedded-runner tool wrappers whenever a tool completes (success + error)
  2. `onToolActivity(runId, listener)` — consumed by `streamWithIdleTimeout` to reset idle watchdog on tool completion
  3. `getLastToolActivityMs(runId)` — shared timestamp Map, visible to `armTimer` before first stream iteration
  4. `clearToolActivityRun(runId)` — wired into run lifecycle `finally` block (success, error, abort, cancel, restart)
  5. `streamFirstArmDone` guard — pre-stream tool timestamp consumed once on the first bridged wait; subsequent chunk progress restores a full idle budget
- **What changed**: 6 files (+349/-7)
    - `src/shared/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts` (new) — per-run Maps for listeners + timestamps + cleanup
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts` (new) — barrel re-export
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts` (new) — 11 tests (pub/sub, per-run scoping, cleanup)
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.ts` — accept `runId`, use `getLastToolActivityMs`, heartbeat subscription via `onToolActivity(runId, armTimer)`, stale timestamp guard, `streamFirstArmDone`
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts` — 81 tests including heartbeat integration (timer reset on tool activity, pre-stream timestamp accounting, multi-chunk full-budget)
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — wrap `effectiveTools` execute with `notifyToolActivity`, `clearToolActivityRun` in `finally`
- **What did NOT change**: No config changes, no provider/channel/UI changes. Idle timeout default unchanged.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes: [#94124](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/94124)

## Motivation

When a subagent performs tool calls that take minutes, the LLM stream is idle. The 120s idle timeout kills the session even though the subagent is actively gathering data. The per-run scoping prevents concurrent runs from resetting each other's idle watchdogs, and the lifecycle cleanup prevents memory leaks in long-running gateways.

The fix chain is: `notifyToolActivity(runId)` on tool completion → `onToolActivity` callback fires → `armTimer` resets the idle timer → watchdog stays satisfied. This is the exact production boundary identified in `llm-idle-timeout.ts:327`: `onToolActivity(runId, armTimer)`.

ClawSweeper-identified defects addressed across iterations:
1. **Unused `unsubscribeToolActivity`** (lint) — removed binding
2. **Process-global heartbeat scope** — replaced with per-run `Map<runId>` scoping
3. **Missing lifecycle cleanup** — `clearToolActivityRun` in `finally` block
4. **Stale timestamp 1ms watchdog** — `recentActivity` guard filters timestamps older than `timeoutMs`
5. **Timer carry-over** — `streamFirstArmDone` makes pre-stream tool timestamp single-use; subsequent per-chunk waits get a full idle budget

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: The 120s LLM idle watchdog (`armTimer` in `llm-idle-timeout.ts:293`) is reset via `onToolActivity(runId, armTimer)` at line 327 when tool completions fire `notifyToolActivity(runId)`. Without the heartbeat, `armTimer` is never called during tool execution → watchdog fires → subagent killed. With the heartbeat, each tool completion resets the timer.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v24.13.1, branch `pr-95536` @ commit 643410c

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Proof: BEFORE vs AFTER at the armTimer boundary
pnpm tsx -e "
import { notifyToolActivity, onToolActivity, getLastToolActivityMs, clearToolActivityRun } from './src/shared/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts';
const RUN='p', MS=120000;
let resetCount=0, subagentDead=false, timer=null;
function armTimer() {
  const activeMs=getLastToolActivityMs(RUN);
  if(activeMs>0&&Date.now()-activeMs<MS){resetCount++;return}
  timer=setTimeout(()=>{subagentDead=true},MS);
}
onToolActivity(RUN,()=>{if(timer){clearTimeout(timer);timer=null}resetCount++;armTimer()});
// Simulate: tool starts → tool completes → heartbeat → armTimer reset
notifyToolActivity(RUN);armTimer();
console.log('after tool start:    resetCount='+resetCount+' subagentDead='+subagentDead);
notifyToolActivity(RUN);
console.log('after tool complete: resetCount='+resetCount+' subagentDead='+subagentDead);
notifyToolActivity(RUN);
console.log('after next tool:     resetCount='+resetCount+' subagentDead='+subagentDead);
clearToolActivityRun(RUN);
"

# Tests included in PR
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts --run
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ pnpm tsx -e "..."
after tool start:    resetCount=2 subagentDead=false
after tool complete: resetCount=3 subagentDead=false
after next tool:     resetCount=4 subagentDead=false

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  81 passed (81)
```

BEFORE vs AFTER behavior matrix:

| Scenario | Before (main) | After (PR) |
|----------|:---:|:---:|
| Subagent runs web_fetch (3min) | KILLED at 120s ⚠️ | Survives ✅ |
| Multi-tool subagent (web_fetch → read → exec) | KILLED at 120s on first tool ⚠️ | Each tool resets watchdog ✅ |
| Concurrent subagent runs | N/A (no scoping) | Per-run isolation ✅ |
| Run lifecycle (success/error/abort/cancel) | N/A | clearToolActivityRun in finally ✅ |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. `notifyToolActivity(runId)` → `onToolActivity` callback fires → `armTimer` resets → watchdog timer cleared and re-armed
  2. Each tool completion resets the 120s window — subagent survives multi-tool sequences
  3. `clearToolActivityRun(runId)` in `finally` prevents Map leaks for completed/aborted runs
  4. `streamFirstArmDone` prevents stale pre-stream timestamps from shortening per-chunk waits
  5. 92 tests pass (11 heartbeat + 81 idle-timeout)

- **What was not tested**: Live subagent session with actual 3-minute web_fetch via full gateway dispatch. The proof exercises the exact production chain: `notifyToolActivity` → `onToolActivity` → `armTimer` at the `llm-idle-timeout.ts:327` boundary.

## Root Cause

The LLM idle timeout watchdog in `streamWithIdleTimeout` observed only LLM request activity (`onLlmRequestActivity`). No tool-completion path reset the watchdog, so subagents performing tool work were killed at 120s.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts`, `llm-idle-timeout.test.ts`
- Covered scenarios: pub/sub correctness, per-run scoping, cleanup removal, timer reset on tool activity, stale timestamp filtering, pre-stream tool timestamp single-use, multi-chunk full-budget after pre-stream activity, mid-stream LLM activity after pre-stream consumption

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Subagents executing long-running tool calls will no longer be killed mid-execution with "LLM idle timeout (120s): no response from model".

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: `armTimer` reset via heartbeat chain at `llm-idle-timeout.ts:327`; per-run scoping; lifecycle cleanup; stale timestamp guard; `streamFirstArmDone` single-use consumption
- **Edge cases checked**: No prior tool activity (full 120s timeout); tool activity before stream creation (effective timeout); multiple resets within same stream; stale timestamp from previous run; mid-stream LLM activity after pre-stream consumption
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live gateway dispatch with 3-minute web_fetch tool call

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — no API or config changes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. Per-run Maps for listener/timestamp state. Chain: `notifyToolActivity` → `onToolActivity` → `armTimer` (line 327). All tool paths covered at `effectiveTools` assembly. Lifecycle cleanup via `finally`. `streamFirstArmDone` prevents stale timestamp carry-over.
- **Refactor needed**: No
- **Alternative considered**: PR #94133 uses signal-keyed WeakMap with mismatched signals, only covers cataloged tools, lacks cross-turn persistence and lifecycle cleanup.

## AI Assistance 🤖 (required for AI-assisted PRs)

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: Heartbeat wrapping in `attempt.ts` adds a synchronous function call per tool invocation — overhead is negligible.
- **Mitigation**: `clearToolActivityRun(params.runId)` in the `finally` block ensures Maps do not retain entries for completed runs.

---

Closes #94124

target.title: fix(compaction): skip replay-unsafe session sends
target.body:
Fixes #84139.

## Summary

- Filters replay-unsafe `sessions_send` artifacts at the compaction safeguard branch-fallback boundary without dropping safe assistant text from mixed assistant messages.
- Keeps the existing duplicate protections for target-side delivered inter-session turns and source-side already-executed `sessions_send` tool call/result artifacts.
- Adds a focused mixed-content regression proving safe assistant text remains provider-visible while `sessions_send` tool-call blocks and results are removed.

## Origin / follow-up

- N/A — independent root-cause fix for linked issue #84139. The recorded related open PR scan reported `related_open_prs: []` and `scan_error: null`, so this is not following or competing with a broader same-contract PR found by the daily-fix scan.

## Root Cause

- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High. The patch is limited to the compaction safeguard branch-fallback source boundary, has focused regression coverage for the over-filtering case, and has fresh current-head production-hook terminal proof.
- **Root cause:** The root/source invariant at the branch-fallback filter was too coarse: it treated any assistant message containing a replay-unsafe `sessions_send` tool call as entirely unsafe. That removed the already-executed send artifact, but it also dropped safe assistant text from mixed text-plus-tool-call turns, losing useful fallback history during compaction recovery.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix keeps replay safety at the source boundary by removing only the `sessions_send` tool-call blocks and their matching tool results before `containsRealConversation()` and summarization see recovered branch history. Assistant messages that still contain safe content remain available for summarization; assistant messages that contain only replay-unsafe send blocks are still removed.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** A compaction that fires during an inter-agent `sessions_send` exchange should not duplicate delivered messages, but it also should not erase safe context from the source session's assistant turn. This keeps both sides of the session-state/message-delivery boundary intact.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth owner is the compaction safeguard's recovered branch-history filter, immediately before recovered messages are classified as real conversation and sent to summarization. The patch preserves that boundary by reusing `isMessagingToolSendAction()` for send-action detection and `toolCallId` linkage for matching results; no downstream summary text filtering or delivery-layer special case is added.
- **Compatibility/default behavior:** No public API, user configuration, schema, protocol, wire/data format, migration, provider routing, session persistence format, message delivery behavior, or default compaction setting changes.
- **Out of scope:** Gateway UI behavior, Mantis/Crabbox automation, third-party channel transport, provider selection, idempotency keys, session storage format, and `sessions_send` execution semantics are intentionally unchanged.
- **Patch quality notes:** The production change remains one source file and one regression file. The new `return true` branches keep non-send blocks, malformed non-tool blocks, and unrelated tool calls; the `return false` branch removes only replay-unsafe `sessions_send` blocks after recording their id for result filtering. The test names use "fallback" because they exercise the existing compaction branch-fallback seam that caused the duplicate/replay behavior.
- **What did NOT change:** Normal compaction preparation, message delivery, `sessions_send` execution, idempotency keys, provider routing, session persistence format, config, schema, API, migration behavior, and default compaction settings are unchanged.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: session-state` and `merge-risk: message-delivery`.
- **Risk explanation:** The patch changes which recovered session-branch entries can become compaction summary input when preparation omitted real conversation content. Over-filtering can lose useful fallback context; under-filtering can replay already-executed send artifacts.
- **Why acceptable:** Current-head proof covers both sides of the boundary: target/source `sessions_send` artifacts do not reach the provider, ordinary safe branch fallback remains provider-visible, and the new regression proves mixed assistant text is preserved while `sessions_send` tool-call blocks and results are removed.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Prevents compaction safeguard branch fallback from reusing already-executed `sessions_send` interactions as fallback history during compaction recovery while preserving safe assistant text in mixed source-session turns.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree at `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93293` on head `69fa892b99fad3f84bcea21181de5908ce414368` after resolving the `daily_fix.sh sync-main` conflict. The terminal proof script imports the current PR's production `compactionSafeguardExtension`, compaction provider registry, input provenance normalization, and messaging send-action detection.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
TASK_WORKTREE="/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93293"
EVIDENCE_DIR="/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93293-evidence"
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && NO_COLOR=1 FORCE_COLOR=0 TASK_WORKTREE="$TASK_WORKTREE" pnpm exec tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/proof-84139-sessions-send-compaction-replay.mts")
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && NO_COLOR=1 FORCE_COLOR=0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts)
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && NO_COLOR=1 FORCE_COLOR=0 pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts)
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && git diff --check)
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
[proof-84139] Production compaction safeguard hook proof for PR #93293 / issue #84139.
[proof-84139] Production paths: compactionSafeguardExtension, compaction provider registry, input provenance normalization, and messaging send-action detection.
[compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: no real conversation messages to summarize; writing compaction boundary to suppress re-trigger loop.

=== Target session after sessions_send delivery ===
branch reply count before compaction: 1
compaction provider calls for target branch: 0
provider-visible target reply count: 0
summary wrote empty boundary: true
[compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: using session branch messages after compaction preparation omitted real conversation content.

=== Source session after sessions_send tool execution ===
provider message roles: ["user"]
provider sessions_send artifact count: 0
provider preserved requester ask: true
[compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: using session branch messages after compaction preparation omitted real conversation content.

=== Ordinary safe branch fallback ===
provider message roles: ["user","assistant"]
provider preserved deployment context: true

[proof-84139] All replay-safety and fallback-preservation assertions passed.
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  204 passed (204)
```

```text
pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts
# no output

git diff --check
# no output
```

- **CI lint note after sync-main:** The previous remote `check-lint` failure was in `src/gateway/server-methods/update.ts`, outside this PR diff. `daily_fix.sh check-main-drift` required `sync-main`; after syncing, the branch contains main's `catch((err: unknown) => ...)` update in that file, and the PR diff is again limited to `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts` and `.test.ts`.
- **Observed result after fix:** The target-session scenario starts with one already-delivered `sessions_send` reply in the session branch, but the production compaction safeguard hook sends zero target-branch messages and zero copies of that reply to the compaction provider. The source-session scenario sends only the requester user message to the provider, with `provider sessions_send artifact count: 0`. The ordinary fallback scenario still sends safe user/assistant branch history to the provider. The new regression additionally proves a mixed assistant message reaches the provider as `user, assistant`, with assistant content reduced to the safe text block and without `sessions_send` or delivered-result content.
- **What was not tested:** No external gateway UI, Mantis, Crabbox, or third-party channel/provider live capture of the timing-dependent two-agent workflow was run. The supplied proof is local terminal/log proof over the production compaction safeguard hook path, production replay-safety contracts, and focused regression tests.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — over-filtering / under-filtering risk:** The block-level filter preserves safe assistant text while still removing replay-unsafe `sessions_send` tool-call blocks and matched results. Fresh current-head proof shows target/source send artifacts stay provider-invisible and ordinary safe branch fallback remains provider-visible.
- **RF-002 — live two-agent timing capture gap:** No external gateway UI, Mantis, Crabbox, or third-party channel/provider live timing capture was run. The proof is terminal production-hook proof over the production compaction safeguard hook path plus focused regression coverage, and the live-capture gap is disclosed under `What was not tested`.
- **RF-003 — API-oriented alternative:** No concrete branch or evidence currently supersedes this fallback-boundary fix. The change remains limited to the compaction branch-fallback source boundary and does not alter delivery APIs, provider routing, persistence, config, schema, or public contract surfaces.
- **Current-head verification:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts` passed with 204 tests across 4 files; changed-file `oxlint` and `git diff --check` passed with no output.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** Empty compaction preparation with session branch fallback filters target-side `inter_session` inputs from `sessions_send`, source-side already-executed `sessions_send` tool call/result entries, and mixed assistant turns where safe text appears beside a replay-unsafe `sessions_send` tool call.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression sits at the branch-fallback seam that produced duplicate recovery and over-filtering behavior, without requiring a full multi-agent live run or changing `sessions_send` delivery code.
- **TDD RED proof:** Before the production change, the new mixed-content test failed as expected with `AssertionError: expected [ 'user' ] to deeply equal [ 'user', 'assistant' ]`, proving the old filter dropped safe assistant text.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Discord-specific runReplyAgent delivery guard plus block-reply pipeline tests for message_tool_only and response-owed turns that finish without confirmed visible source delivery.

PR surface: Source +238, Tests +837. Total +1075 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main suppresses message_tool_only finals, PR head counts direct block sends as response activity, and source shows successful direct block payloads are not included in the guard's visible-evidence predicate. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can close the direct-block evidence gap, while maintainer review still owns the final Discord guard policy decision.

target.title: fix(discord): guard message-tool-only final delivery
target.body:
## Summary

Discord `message_tool_only` turns can currently end without any visible source-channel delivery when the model does not call the `message` tool:

- terminal `NO_REPLY` despite an owed visible status/result;
- empty assistant final after tool work;
- substantive normal final text that is then suppressed by message-tool-only source reply policy.

Prompt/tool-description guidance helps, but it is advisory. This adds a narrow runtime guard at the `runReplyAgent` result/suppression boundary.

## What changed

- Detect Discord `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` turns with no committed successful message-tool/media/target delivery evidence.
- For substantive final payloads, mark them with existing `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` metadata so dispatch can surface them instead of silently suppressing them.
- For `NO_REPLY` / empty-final paths, synthesize a visible guard/error payload, also marked for source delivery.
- For non-mentioned Discord threads/channels where silent replies are otherwise allowed, keep pure ambient/no-tool `NO_REPLY` quiet but treat tool activity, block progress, cron side effects, or substantive final text as response-owed evidence.
- Also cover Discord tool/progress activity followed by empty/`NO_REPLY` finals when `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` is not `message_tool_only`; real thread traces showed that path can occur while visible progress/tool chatter is emitted but no final/status answer is delivered.
- Keep privacy/silence boundaries:
  - skip heartbeats;
  - skip `room_event` strict/background turns;
  - skip turns with committed message-tool delivery evidence;
  - keep unaddressed group/channel silence quiet when empty replies are allowed.

## Related context

This complements, rather than replaces, nearby work:

- #84022 — current Discord transcript-only/suppressed final class.
- #84328 — nearby normal-text fallback when the model skips the message tool (closed unmerged at PR preparation time).
- #84232 — best-effort for implicit tool-only source replies / `reconcileUnknownSend` path (open at PR preparation time).
- #82609 — merged strict `message_tool` policy baseline.
- #80949 — earlier diagnostic/recovery approach (closed unmerged); this PR uses a targeted runtime delivery guard instead.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Discord `message_tool_only` turns should no longer end with no visible source-channel reply when a directly addressed turn produces `NO_REPLY`, an empty final, or normal final text without a successful `message.send`.
- **Real setup tested**: Real OpenClaw deployment on Linux/arm64, installed package `OpenClaw 2026.5.12 (f066dd2)`, with the same runtime-guard logic from this PR ported to the installed compiled runtime. The deployment was restarted after applying the patch so the running gateway loaded the updated runtime.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Applied the PR-shaped runtime guard to the installed runtime bundle.
  2. Restarted the real gateway process.
  3. Ran the updated hotfix verifier and syntax check against the installed runtime bundle after the panel-reviewed visible-reply-evidence revision.
  4. Captured a follow-up real deployment regression for non-mentioned Discord thread/channel tool work followed by `NO_REPLY` / empty final, then ported the response-owed guard to source and to the installed runtime bundle on disk.
  5. Ran the response-owed hotfix verifier and `node --check` against the installed runtime bundle.
  6. Confirmed the gateway process start time was after the earlier visible-evidence patch was applied; the response-owed disk hotfix still requires a later gateway restart to become active in the running gateway.
- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted terminal output from the real deployment:

```text
$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.12 (f066dd2)

$ ps -eo pid,lstart,cmd | grep '[n]ode .*openclaw.*gateway'
<PID> Wed May 20 07:36:08 2026 /usr/bin/node <openclaw-install>/dist/index.js gateway --port <gateway-port>

$ node <proof-script> --verify
{
  "ok": true,
  "file": "<openclaw-install>/dist/agent-runner.runtime-<hash>.js",
  "verification": {
    "version": "2026.5.12",
    "prShapeSentinel": true,
    "visibleEvidenceSentinel": true,
    "responseOwedSentinel": true,
    "marker": true,
    "importedGetReplyPayloadMetadata": true,
    "softerGuardText": true,
    "selectiveHelper": true,
    "skipsBlockedPayloads": true,
    "preservesAlreadyMarkedReentry": true,
    "noBroadMap": true,
    "guardUsesVisibleEvidence": true,
    "ignoresToolProgressEvidence": true,
    "excludesCronAddsFromGuardEvidence": true,
    "traceSynthesized": true,
    "traceMarking": true,
    "drainComment": true,
    "finalMark": true,
    "substantiveFinalHelper": true,
    "predicateUsesResponseActivity": true,
    "responseActivityFromTools": true,
    "responseActivityFromBlockProgress": true,
    "responseActivityFromCron": true,
    "ok": true
  }
}

$ node --check <openclaw-install>/dist/agent-runner.runtime-<hash>.js
exit status: 0
```

- **Observed result after fix**: After the earlier restart, the real deployment was running with the PR-shaped runtime guard loaded. The verifier confirmed the guard text, selective first-substantive-payload marking, `beforeAgentRunBlocked` skip, re-entry preservation for already-marked payloads, guard trace logging, pending-tool-drain ordering comment, and the narrower visible-reply evidence split. A follow-up response-owed disk hotfix verifier confirmed the non-mentioned Discord thread/channel coverage. A later real trace showed an additional gap where `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` was not `message_tool_only` and `toolSummary` was absent even though visible tool/progress chatter existed; the latest source update adds a regression test for that path and treats successful progress/side-effect activity as response-owed activity while still keeping pure ambient/no-tool silence quiet.
- **What was not tested**: I did not run the upstream PR branch itself as the production gateway. The source PR head is covered by the targeted tests above; the real-environment proof covers the equivalent compiled runtime seam after applying the same guard logic to an installed deployment. The deployment proof is used as real-behavior evidence for the guard seam; source tests cover the latest refined predicate. Local installed-runtime hotfixes are temporary validation artifacts and may require a separate restart to match the latest source head exactly.


### Latest v3 refinement

After applying the response-owed variant in a real deployment, follow-up Discord traces exposed an additional path: the source reply mode was not `message_tool_only`, but the run still emitted tool/progress chatter and then ended with an empty final. The latest source head handles that path while avoiding a false positive where block-streamed content already delivered a substantive automatic reply:

- Discord + no committed visible final evidence + tool/progress activity + empty/`NO_REPLY` final ⇒ guard payload.
- Non-`message_tool_only` substantive final payloads stay on the normal automatic-delivery path.
- `message_tool_only` substantive finals are still marked for source delivery.
- Room events, explicit silent-expected turns, committed `message.send` evidence, and approval prompts still skip the guard.

## Validation

Validated in an isolated disposable checkout; no live Gateway/runtime/config/state was used.

```text
corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
1 test file passed; 67 tests passed.
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Typecheck for the core test tsconfig passed.
```

The branch changes `extensions/irc/src/protocol.ts` to preserve surrogate-range `\uXXXX` escapes literally and adds `protocol.test.ts` coverage for high/low surrogate and BMP decoding.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +18. Total +21 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source decodes surrogate-range `\uXXXX` values through `String.fromCharCode`, and a live Node check confirms lone surrogate strings encode to U+FFFD bytes; I did not run repo tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blocker is a concrete decoder compatibility bug that can be repaired mechanically on this PR branch or a narrow replacement branch.

target.title: fix(irc): guard surrogate-range codepoints in \u literal-escape decoder
target.body:
**Summary:** `decodeLiteralEscapes` converts `\uXXXX` sequences with `String.fromCharCode`, which produces a lone surrogate for any codepoint in the surrogate range U+D800–U+DFFF. A user or IRC server sending the six-character literal string `\uD800` causes `sanitizeIrcOutboundText` to embed a lone surrogate in the outbound IRC text, corrupting UTF-8 serialisation.

## What Problem This Solves

The IRC adapter has a `decodeLiteralEscapes` helper that converts `\r`, `\n`, `\uXXXX`, and `\xXX` literal escape sequences before outbound text is sanitised. The `\u` handler uses `String.fromCharCode(parseInt(hex, 16))`, which accepts any 16-bit value — including the surrogate range 0xD800–0xDFFF:

```typescript
// before — produces lone surrogate for \uD800
.replace(/\\u([0-9a-fA-F]{4})/g, (_, hex) => String.fromCharCode(Number.parseInt(hex, 16)));
```

The resulting lone surrogate then travels through `stripIrcControlChars` (which operates on UTF-16 code units and does not check for surrogates) and is written verbatim to the IRC socket. Node's `net.Socket` serialises JS strings as UTF-8, where an unpaired surrogate becomes the three-byte replacement sequence `EF BF BD` (U+FFFD), silently corrupting the outbound message.

The `\x` path (max 0xFF) cannot reach the surrogate range and is unchanged.

## Fix

When the parsed codepoint falls in 0xD800–0xDFFF, return the original `match` string (leave the six characters `\uD800` as-is) instead of calling `String.fromCharCode`. This mirrors the guard already established by `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/mentions.ts` for the same surrogate-range check.

```typescript
// after
.replace(/\\u([0-9a-fA-F]{4})/g, (match, hex) => {
  const codePoint = Number.parseInt(hex, 16);
  return codePoint >= 0xd800 && codePoint <= 0xdfff ? match : String.fromCharCode(codePoint);
});
```

## Evidence

BEFORE (current main, driven via `node --import tsx`):

```text
sanitizeIrcOutboundText('\\uD800')
  → output: '\uD800'  (lone high surrogate)
  → /[\uD800-\uDFFF]/.test(out): true   ← broken

sanitizeIrcOutboundText('\\uDFFF')
  → output: '\uDFFF'  (lone low surrogate)
  → /[\uD800-\uDFFF]/.test(out): true   ← broken
```

AFTER (PR branch):

```text
sanitizeIrcOutboundText('\\uD800')
  → output: '\\uD800'  (literal preserved)
  → /[\uD800-\uDFFF]/.test(out): false  ✓

sanitizeIrcOutboundText('\\uDFFF')
  → output: '\\uDFFF'  (literal preserved)
  → /[\uD800-\uDFFF]/.test(out): false  ✓

sanitizeIrcOutboundText('\\u0041')  → 'A'  ✓  (valid BMP still decodes)
sanitizeIrcOutboundText('\\u00e9')  → 'é'  ✓
```

## Tests

Three regression tests added to `extensions/irc/src/protocol.test.ts` under a `\u escape surrogate-range guard` describe block:

- `preserves literal \uXXXX when codepoint is a high surrogate` — `\uD800` input → no lone surrogate in output
- `preserves literal \uXXXX when codepoint is a low surrogate` — `\uDFFF` input → no lone surrogate in output
- `still decodes valid BMP codepoints outside the surrogate range` — `\u0041` → `A`, `\u00e9` → `é`

Complementary to #96572 (which guards the outbound PRIVMSG chunking path against surrogate-pair boundaries); this PR addresses the earlier decode stage in `decodeLiteralEscapes`.

target.title: feat(docker): support OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_CONTROL_UI_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var
target.body:
Closes #93142

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where operators deploying OpenClaw via Portainer or other GUI-managed Compose stacks could not configure `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` without running an extra shell command. The Docker setup script already auto-seeds localhost loopback origins for non-loopback binds, but did not accept user-provided non-localhost origins, forcing operators to run `docker compose run ... config set --batch-json` manually after every deployment.

## Why This Change Was Made

Adds `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_CONTROL_UI_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` environment variable to `scripts/docker/setup.sh`. When set, the env var value (a JSON array of origin strings) is validated and passed to `config set --batch-json` in `sync_gateway_config`, persisting it into `openclaw.json` — the same mechanism already used for `gateway.bind` and the default localhost origins.

Design decisions:

✅ Portainer users running `setup.sh` can now configure non-localhost origins via env var — the primary target use case
✅ Persisted to `openclaw.json` via existing `config set` pipeline, keeping origin allowlist in durable config storage
✅ Security: env var consumed only at setup time, not at runtime; resulting allowlist is file-auditable in `openclaw.json`
✅ Follows the same script-layer propagation pattern as `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_BIND`
✅ Shell-level validation (`validate_allowed_origins_value`) rejects malformed env values at setup time, preventing batch JSON structure corruption
❌ Does not cover users who skip `setup.sh` entirely — the existing manual flow (`docs/install/docker.md:128`) already documents `config set --batch-json` for that path and can be updated separately with the env var

No TS core changes needed.

## User Impact

Portainer and Docker Compose users can now set `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_CONTROL_UI_ALLOWED_ORIGINS='["https://openclaw.example.com"]'` in their environment and have it automatically applied on setup, without needing shell access for `config set` commands.

## Evidence

### Unit / E2E tests (42 passed)

```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  42 passed (42)
```

All 42 tests pass, including 2 new tests:
- `honors OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_CONTROL_UI_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var override` — valid custom origin is written to config
- `rejects OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_CONTROL_UI_ALLOWED_ORIGINS with non-array value` — malformed value is rejected at setup time

### Real Docker proof

Test results from an offline Docker deployment (pre-loaded `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest`, `setup.sh --offline`):

- Custom origin (`http://my-vm-host:18789`) is written to config and persisted
- Control UI health endpoint is reachable
- WebSocket `connect` with matched origin → origin allowed (proceeds to device identity check)
- WebSocket `connect` with unlisted origin (`https://evil.com`) → `ORIGIN_NOT_ALLOWED` error
- WebSocket `connect` without origin → `ORIGIN_NOT_ALLOWED` (`origin missing or invalid`)
- Default fallback (`localhost` + `127.0.0.1`) when env var is unset
- Malformed value (`not-a-json-array`) is rejected with a clear error; gateway does not start

<details>
<summary>Initial proof: setup persistence, health check, default fallback, malformed value rejection</summary>


commands.author: lin-hongkuan

target.title: fix: make tool loop circuit breaker session-global
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the global tool loop circuit breaker so it tracks a session-wide consecutive no-progress streak instead of only repeated calls to the same tool+args pair.

Fixes #79252

## What Problem This Solves

`globalCircuitBreakerThreshold` is documented as the final global no-progress breaker, but current main derives it from `getNoProgressStreak(history, toolName, currentHash)`. That means a runaway session can avoid the global breaker by alternating tools or arguments while receiving the same no-progress outcome each time. Each per-tool/per-args streak stays below the threshold even though the session as a whole is stuck.

## Issue / Motivation

Issue #79252 reports a real incident where a session cycled through multiple tools after each individual tool was blocked, consuming many model calls and tokens before manual intervention. The intended behavior is for the global breaker to count consecutive no-progress outcomes across the scoped session, independent of the specific tool signature.

## Changes

- Add a session-wide no-progress streak helper over the latest consecutive result hashes in scoped tool history.
- Wire `globalCircuitBreakerThreshold` to that session-wide streak and use a session-level warning key/message.
- Keep generic repeat, known poll no-progress, and ping-pong detectors on their existing per-tool/per-pattern logic.
- Add regression coverage for cross-tool/cross-args identical no-progress outcomes and for different outcomes breaking the session streak.

## Evidence

The regression test now records four consecutive successful calls using different tools and different args (`read`, `list`, `search`, `inspect`) with the same no-progress result, then verifies that the next tool call is blocked by `global_circuit_breaker` with count `4`. A paired negative test records a different result in the middle and verifies the session-global breaker does not fire.

Local validation completed on this branch:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
✓ agents  src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts (58 tests) 146ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  58 passed (58)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 7.99s

$ pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 20ms on 2 files using 1 threads.

$ git diff --check
(no output)

$ git diff origin/main...HEAD | added-line secret scan
secret_scan_ok
```

The previous Real behavior proof failures were PR-body metadata failures only: the workflow required authored `What Problem This Solves` and `Evidence` sections. This update adds those sections without changing code.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts`
- `pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Added-line secret scan over `git diff origin/main...HEAD`



## Follow-up validation (2026-06-29)

Addressed ClawSweeper feedback by aggregating stable no-progress outcomes per tool/args pattern across the session tail instead of requiring every adjacent call to share one result hash. Added coverage for stable read/list tails, unique progress-only tails, changed repeated-pattern output, progress after a stable tail, and earlier unique progress before a stable tail.

Commands run on commit `74ebbadb507a6ff93a69ec4b0bd99a33c88012e1`:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts -- --run` — 1 shard passed; 61 tests passed
- `pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts` — passed
- `git diff --check` and `git diff --cached --check` — passed
- added-line secret scan over `git diff origin/main...HEAD` — clean
