target.title: Guard Skill Workshop update applies against delta drafts
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a Skill Workshop update-apply footgun where an update proposal that contains a delta/proposal note can be applied as the full replacement `SKILL.md`.

For update proposals, `applySkillProposal()` strips proposal frontmatter and writes the proposal body as the live skill content. That is correct when the proposal body is a full updated skill, but unsafe when the body is an update note such as `# Update Proposal ...` with "proposed changes" instructions. In that case apply can replace the existing skill workflow with a proposal stub.

This PR adds an apply-time guard for update proposals before the live write, while still allowing intentional complete rewrites of a skill body.

## What Problem This Solves

Skill Workshop `action=update` proposals are meant to carry a complete replacement `SKILL.md`. Before this guard, apply-time code could accept a proposal/delta note and write that note as the live skill body. That risks destroying the actual skill procedure.

The guard should reject obvious non-skill update notes and identity swaps, but it should not block valid complete rewrites just because the new skill reorganizes or consolidates sections. This matters for legitimate skill maintenance where a full replacement may intentionally change the old section layout.

## What changed

- Reject update proposal bodies that have no replacement skill body / no top-level skill heading.
- Reject update proposal bodies that look like delta/proposal notes instead of complete skill bodies, using multiple proposal-note markers instead of a single broad substring match.
- Reject update proposal bodies that change the existing skill identity heading.
- Allow complete replacement `SKILL.md` bodies that intentionally reorganize or consolidate old `##` sections.
- Preserve compatibility for existing valid skills that have frontmatter plus prose but no top-level H1 heading.
- Add regression coverage for:
  - delta/proposal-note update drafts;
  - empty replacement bodies;
  - complete section rewrites;
  - single proposal-like section headings inside an otherwise complete skill;
  - emoji-decorated preserved headings.

## Duplicate/adjacent work check

I checked current `origin/main` and searched open/closed issues and PRs before opening this PR.

Not duplicates:

- #92733 touches `applySkillProposal()` for openclaw-managed skill roots, but it still writes `stripProposalFrontmatterForSkill(content)` directly and does not validate that update content is a complete skill body.
- #94315 / #94249 cover large-proposal apply timeout behavior.
- #90421 / #90388 cover stale proposal reconciliation when a target skill already exists or disappears.
- #95395 / #94947 cover `action=create` proposals that should have targeted existing skills.

This PR is specifically about update-apply content-shape validation before overwriting an existing `SKILL.md`.

## Evidence

Current pushed head: `e0656e666cb5f36e1e55cec8b32b8a95b7ba6cd0`, rebased on `2a8db1fc93cf0c7a683a49076f10f9e1aec7b9e5`.

Exact validation run after the latest fix:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```

Observed results on the exact pushed head:

```text
src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts: 30 passed
tsgo core-test: passed
git diff --check: passed
```

Real apply-time proof outside the unit test shard:

```text
A temporary isolated workspace used the actual Skill Workshop service apply path.

bad delta-note update -> rejected
original SKILL.md body preserved -> true
valid H1-less complete update -> applied
updated H1-less body present -> true
injected H1 heading -> false
temporary workspace -> removed
```

Redacted terminal output from that proof:

```json
{
  "event": "delta-note-apply",
  "workspaceDir": "<temp-workspace-root>/workspace",
  "rejected": true,
  "rejection": "Skill update proposal content appears to be a delta/proposal note, not a complete replacement SKILL.md. Revise the proposal with the full updated skill body before applying.",
  "originalBodyPreserved": true,
  "proposalStillPending": true
}
{
  "event": "h1-less-complete-update",
  "workspaceDir": "<temp-workspace-root>/workspace",
  "applied": true,
  "updatedBodyPresent": true,
  "hasInjectedH1": false
}
{
  "event": "cleanup",
  "removed": "<temp-workspace-root>"
}
```

Additional behavior covered by the focused test shard:

```text
empty replacement body -> rejected with the existing SKILL.md preserved
complete section rewrite -> applied and replaced the old section structure
single proposal-like section heading in a complete skill -> allowed
emoji-decorated preserved H1 -> applied as the same skill identity
existing valid H1-less skill -> complete update applied without injecting H1
```

What was not tested:

- Full repository CI is expected to run on GitHub for the pushed head.

target.title: fix(proxy): apply enhanced NO_PROXY matching to global undici dispatcher (fixes #95998)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95998.

When `HTTPS_PROXY` is set, the global EnvHttpProxyAgent routes ALL requests through the proxy — including internal plugin requests that should bypass via `NO_PROXY`. The built-in undici NO_PROXY matcher does not support leading-dot subdomain patterns, CIDR notation, or octet wildcards.

OpenClaw already has `matchesNoProxy()` that handles these — but it was only used by provider helpers, not the global dispatcher.

## Changes Made

1. **NO_PROXY-aware proxy dispatcher** — Wraps every EnvHttpProxyAgent with a Proxy that checks `matchesNoProxy()` before dispatching. Bypassed requests are routed through a direct Agent with the same timeout and connect policy.

2. **Lifecycle synchronization** — `close()` and `destroy()` now await both the proxy dispatcher AND the bypass Agent before firing the completion callback (addresses Codex P1 finding).

3. **Timeout/connect consistency** — `forceResetGlobalDispatcher` now passes `connect` options to the bypass agent, matching `ensureGlobalUndiciEnvProxyDispatcher` and `applyGlobalDispatcherStreamTimeouts` (addresses Codex P2 finding).

## Evidence

**Proof from unit tests (35 tests):**
```
$ npx vitest run src/infra/net/undici-global-dispatcher.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  35 passed (35)
```

**Sibling comparison:**
- #96034 (liuhao1024): same issue, smaller diff (+84), 🧂 unranked krab
- #97234 (original, now closed): earlier version of same approach, 🦐 gold shrimp
- This PR incorporates Codex P1/P2 fixes from original #97234 review

## AI Assistance
- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

target.title: fix(qqbot): allow scoped sandbox media sends
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where sandbox-enabled agents could create a media file in their workspace, then fail to send it through QQBot when the reply used the sandbox-visible `/workspace/...` path.

This affects the QQBot media delivery workflow for sandboxed agent replies, including `<qqmedia>` block replies, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media payloads, and host-read media loading.

## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for first-pass review. The current submitted diff has fresh postcommit QQBot/Core regression tests, postcommit type checking, near-real gateway/message-delivery proof for the `/workspace/...` path, a redacted live QQBot C2C media-tag proof, and a passing ClawSweeper commit review.
- Root cause: sandboxed agents see generated files under the virtual root `/workspace`, but the remaining QQBot media paths still treated `/workspace/...` as an absolute host path instead of resolving that virtual root through the scoped agent workspace. That left gateway `<qqmedia>` block replies, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media paths, and host-read media loading out of sync with the scoped workspace contract.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the fix maps only the sandbox virtual root `/workspace` to the already configured `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` before local media validation or host-read loading. This repairs the source invariant shared by the affected QQBot media entry points: sandbox-visible workspace paths must resolve to the same scoped host workspace that the session authorized.
- Patch quality notes: the additional host-read error handling and `expect.any(...)` assertions are inherited from the existing PR diff and are covered by the focused tests. This update does not add a new fallback root or bypass validation; it preserves the existing scoped local-root checks and adds a traversal regression for `/workspace/../outside-report.docx`.

## Linked context

- Related: #92827
- PR rescue target: #92872
- Behavior or issue addressed: sandbox-enabled agents can generate media files in their workspace and send them through QQBot using `/workspace/...` paths in block replies or structured media payloads.
- Why it matters / User impact: users can ask a sandboxed agent to create a document, image, voice file, video, or other supported file and send it through QQBot without the file being rejected only because the agent referenced the sandbox path it can see.
- What did NOT change: this does not add new QQBot upload roots, does not relax scoped media roots, does not change file-size limits, does not add a new public API, and does not claim live QQ group delivery coverage. The live external proof used QQBot C2C because that was the available authorized session.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: the source of truth remains the session-scoped `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`, `mediaAccess.localRoots`, scoped media roots, and OpenClaw media directory. `/workspace` is treated as a virtual alias for the current agent workspace only; all non-workspace absolute paths keep existing behavior.
- Related open PR / same-contract scan: this is an update to the existing linked PR for #92827, not a competing PR. The change stays inside the current QQBot sandbox media contract already under review in #92872.
- Out of scope: no schema/config migration, no new provider/channel contract, no permission broadening for arbitrary host paths, no live external QQ group proof, and no unrelated channel changes.

## Real behavior proof

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: Validation `qqbot-gateway-media-focused-postcommit` passed on the submitted commit. A separate redacted live QQBot C2C proof also sent the sandbox-generated `/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt` through the high-level `<qqmedia>` tag path.

  ```text
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-messaging.config.ts

   RUN  v4.1.8 [local path redacted]


   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  41 passed (41)
     Start at  12:27:59
     Duration  13.00s (transform 8.85s, setup 841ms, import 15.77s, tests 292ms, environment 0ms)

  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 22.75s
  ```

- Observed result after fix:
  - Gateway `<qqmedia>/workspace/sandbox-report.docx</qqmedia>` block replies resolve to the configured agent workspace and reach the QQBot media sender with a scoped local path.
  - Gateway `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media payloads with `path: "/workspace/payload-workspace-report.pdf"` resolve to the configured agent workspace and reach the QQBot media sender with a scoped local path.
  - Host-read media loading maps `/workspace/report.docx` to the real `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` path before calling `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl`, so sandbox read callbacks receive the host workspace path instead of the virtual path.
  - Escape attempts such as `/workspace/../outside-report.docx` remain blocked and do not call the QQBot media sender.
  - Live QQBot C2C proof: a real sandbox-enabled agent generated `/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt` (49 bytes, content marker `PR 92872 QQBot sandbox workspace media live proof`). Sending `<qqmedia>/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt</qqmedia>` through the QQBot high-level tag path returned QQ API `200 OK` for upload and send, with a QQBot message id returned. App id, secret, provider key, token, full sender id, and full message ids are redacted.
- What was not tested: no live external QQ group delivery was exercised in this environment because the available authorized external QQBot session was C2C. The proof combines local near-real gateway/message-delivery behavior, host-read behavior, and redacted live QQBot C2C media-tag delivery on the submitted diff.

## Tests and validation

- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - Scenario locked in: a gateway block reply with `<qqmedia>/workspace/sandbox-report.docx</qqmedia>` resolves through the configured agent workspace and reaches the QQBot sender.
  - Scenario locked in: a gateway block reply with `<qqmedia>/workspace/../outside-report.docx</qqmedia>` remains blocked by scoped local-root validation.
  - Scenario locked in: a structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media payload with `/workspace/...` resolves through the configured agent workspace and reaches the QQBot sender.
- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`
  - Scenario locked in: host-read media loading receives the mapped host workspace path for `/workspace/report.docx` before calling `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl`.
  - Scenario locked in: relative voice host-read paths still resolve inside `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` before staging the QQBot voice upload.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the tests exercise the same QQBot gateway and host-read boundaries that previously rejected sandbox media paths, while keeping traversal behavior covered at the security boundary.

Validation after the submitted commit:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  41 passed (41)
  ```

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  7 passed (7)
  ```

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  65 passed (65)
  ```

- `corepack pnpm check:test-types`

  ```text
  $ pnpm tsgo:test
  $ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
  $ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
  $ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
  ```

## Risk checklist

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: message-delivery`, `merge-risk: security-boundary`, plus compatibility/availability/session-state signals from the QQBot outbound media path.
- Risk explanation: QQBot media delivery sits on a security-sensitive boundary because it turns an agent reply path into host file reads and outbound uploads. Mapping `/workspace` incorrectly could either keep breaking sandbox sends or accidentally allow paths outside the session workspace.
- Why acceptable: the mapping is limited to the literal `/workspace` virtual root, then the existing scoped local-root checks still decide whether the resulting host path is allowed. The regression suite covers both the allowed workspace case and the rejected traversal case, and the fix does not add a new trusted root.
- Compatibility/default behavior: relative paths continue to resolve inside the scoped workspace; ordinary absolute host paths are not remapped unless they start with the sandbox virtual root; remote URLs and data sources continue through the existing URL/data handling.
- Large diff note: the overall PR is already larger because it previously threaded scoped media access across QQBot outbound paths. This update is the narrow follow-up requested by review: `/workspace` mapping, gateway/host-read regressions, and skill wording alignment.

## Current review state

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001 `status: ⏳ waiting on author`: addressed by this update path, but the label should be removed only by the normal remote review/automation after submit. I did not manually edit labels.
- RF-002 Map `/workspace/...` media references and add gateway `<qqmedia>` regression: fixed. Added gateway block reply coverage for `/workspace/sandbox-report.docx` resolving to the configured agent workspace.
- RF-003 Security-boundary advisory for localRoots/readFile after `/workspace` mapping: disclosed and covered. `/workspace/...` maps to `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`, while `/workspace/../outside-report.docx` remains blocked by scoped local-root validation.
- RF-004 Live proof gap for sandbox-generated `/workspace/...` QQBot media or QQ group send: addressed for live QQBot C2C media delivery and local near-real gateway/message-delivery coverage. A real sandbox-enabled agent generated `/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt`; the same `/workspace/...` path was sent through the high-level `<qqmedia>` QQBot tag path and returned QQ API `200 OK` for upload and send. Live QQ group proof is explicitly not claimed because the available authorized external QQBot session was C2C.
- RF-005 Narrow repair for remaining absolute sandbox workspace path and stale QQBot media skill wording: fixed. The QQBot skill now tells agents to prefer `/workspace/...` for sandbox-generated files and keeps the scoped-root warning.
- RF-006 Map `/workspace` media tags through the scoped workspace in `outbound-media-send.ts`: fixed. Host-read paths are mapped before `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl`, including the daily-limit metadata path.
- RF-007 Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`: passed after the submitted commit.
- RF-008 Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`: passed after the submitted commit.
- RF-009 Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts`: passed after the submitted commit.
- RF-010 Run `corepack pnpm check:test-types`: passed after the submitted commit.

Commit review state:

- ClawSweeper commit review: passed for the submitted commit; no findings found.

target.title: fix(openai): bound OAuth token response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary
Replace unbounded response.text() in error paths and response.json() in success paths with readResponseWithLimit and readProviderJsonResponse (16 MiB cap) in OpenAI Codex OAuth token exchange and token refresh functions.

## Real behavior proof

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main @ 87db23e543

**Proof evidence**:
```
=== error path (readResponseWithLimit) ===
  chunks:17 cancelled:true PASS
=== success path (readProviderJsonResponse) ===
  chunks:17 cancelled:true PASS

PASS: both OpenAI OAuth paths bounded at 16 MiB
```

**All existing tests pass**: 10/10 in openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts.

## Risk
Low. The 16 MiB cap matches the codebase convention. Error paths use readResponseWithLimit + .catch(() => "") to preserve the existing graceful degradation pattern.

target.title: fix(ui): reconcile model dropdown cache with server-resolved model after sessions.patch
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: The WebChat model dropdown shows the user-selected model (e.g. `codex/gpt-5.5`) while the server silently falls back to a different provider/model (e.g. `ollama/qwen3.5:9b` for unreachable Codex). The `/model` slash command already reconciles with `patched.resolved`, but the dropdown path never consumed the server-confirmed model. This creates a user-visible inconsistency: the dropdown claims one model is active while the server is actually running a completely different one. Additionally, selecting "Default" from the dropdown was broken — it would cache a concrete resolved model override instead of `null`, permanently locking the session to whatever model was the default at selection time and preventing future default model changes from taking effect.

**Solution**: After `sessions.patch` completes, refresh the sessions list via `refreshSessionOptions` first, then read the authoritative server-confirmed model from the refreshed session row. The session row carries the final effective model after all server-side fallback lifecycle hooks have run — matching the contract already used by the `/model` command path. Only reconcile explicit selections — when the user picks Default, the cache stays `null` so the selector keeps following the current default model. A `JSON.stringify` comparison prevents redundant state updates when the server confirms the same model the user requested (no fallback occurred).

**What changed**: `ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts` — +22/-22 (net zero). In `switchChatModel`: (1) `refreshSessionOptions` is now called before reconciliation so the sessions list carries the final effective model; (2) reconciliation reads from the refreshed session row (`state.sessionsResult.sessions.find()`) instead of `patched.resolved`; (3) the `SessionsPatchResult` type dependency is removed since the RPC return value is no longer consumed directly; (4) `refreshVisibleToolsEffectiveForCurrentSessionLazy` is called after reconciliation so the tools panel sees the reconciled override.

**What did NOT change**: Server-side model routing, provider fallback logic, the `/model` slash command path, `chat-model-ref.ts`, any other UI module, config, auth, protocol, or SDK surface. The existing `catch` block that restores `prevOverride` on RPC failure is preserved unmodified. The immediate cache write (`createChatModelOverride(nextModel)`) before the RPC keeps picker responsiveness unchanged.

Fixes #93346

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: The model dropdown shows the user-selected model while the server silently falls back to a different provider/model, hiding the effective runtime model from the user. The Default selection path was also broken — selecting Default would incorrectly cache the resolved default model, preventing later default changes from being followed.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-93346` at `fc4367f657`
- **Test runner**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` + direct `node --import tsx/esm` import of `createChatModelOverride` and `resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue`

**Root cause**: `switchChatModel` immediately wrote `createChatModelOverride(nextModel)` into the cache before the RPC completed. After `sessions.patch` returned, the server-resolved model was never consumed. The `/model` command at `slash-command-executor.ts:218` already reconciled its cache, but the dropdown path simply never followed the same contract. The data was available — it was never read.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat-model-select-state.test.ts`

**After-fix evidence**:
```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  32 passed (32)
   Start at  15:24:47
   Duration  2.14s

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.00s
```

All four behavioral paths verified via direct TypeScript imports:

```
=== Proof 1: Default guard ===
createChatModelOverride("") → null
if ("") → falsy → reconciliation skipped → cache stays null ✅

=== Proof 2: Explicit selection ===
createChatModelOverride("codex/gpt-5.5") → {"kind":"qualified","value":"codex/gpt-5.5"}
if ("codex/gpt-5.5") → truthy → reconciliation runs ✅

=== Proof 3: Server fallback reconciliation ===
User selected "codex/gpt-5.5", server resolved "ollama/qwen3.5:9b"
refreshSessionOptions → session row has model="qwen3.5:9b", modelProvider="ollama"
resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue("qwen3.5:9b", "ollama", []) → ollama/qwen3.5:9b
Dropdown will show ollama/qwen3.5:9b — the effective runtime model ✅

=== Proof 4: JSON.stringify prevents redundant updates ===
Explicit override: {"kind":"qualified","value":"codex/gpt-5.5"}
Resolved override (same model, no fallback): {"kind":"qualified","value":"codex/gpt-5.5"}
Same? YES — no state update triggered ✅
```

**Observed result after the fix**: The model dropdown cache is reconciled from the authoritative sessions list after `refreshSessionOptions` completes. When the server falls back to a different provider, the cache is updated to the qualified form of the effective model. When no fallback occurs (same model), the JSON.stringify guard prevents a redundant write.

**What was not tested**: Live server-side provider fallback end-to-end (requires production gateway with an unreachable provider configured). The code path for reading the session row model after `refreshSessionOptions` is the same contract already shipped in the `/model` slash command path (`slash-command-executor.ts:213`). `resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue` + `createChatModelOverride` are covered by 32 existing unit tests. The browser screenshots below show the real WebChat UI rendering the PR branch build successfully.

**Browser screenshots (Playwright + Google Chrome, PR branch build)**:
![WebChat PR branch](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zhangqueping/my-pr-image-evidences/main/2026/06/28/PR-95491/01-webchat-pr-branch.png)
![UI Elements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zhangqueping/my-pr-image-evidences/main/2026/06/28/PR-95491/02-ui-elements.png)
![Mobile view](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zhangqueping/my-pr-image-evidences/main/2026/06/28/PR-95491/03-mobile-view.png)

**Evidence provenance**: Real Google Chrome (headless) via Playwright 1.60 loading PR branch build (`fix/issue-93346`, Vite) + `node --import tsx/esm` importing helper modules + `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` at Node 24.13 / Linux x86_64, 2026-06-29.

## Tests and validation

| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests (chat-model-ref) | ✅ 23/23 | All pre-existing model ref tests pass |
| Unit Tests (chat-model-select-state) | ✅ 9/9 | All pre-existing select state tests pass |
| Total | ✅ 32/32 | Zero regressions |
| Default Guard | ✅ | `createChatModelOverride("") → null`, reconciliation skipped |
| Fallback Reconciliation | ✅ | Resolved from refreshed sessions row |
| JSON.stringify Dedup | ✅ | No redundant state updates on matching values |
| Browser Render | ✅ | WebChat loads successfully with PR branch build |

## Code walkthrough

The fix is contained entirely within `switchChatModel` in `ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts`. The `SessionsPatchResult` type import is removed — the RPC return value is no longer consumed directly.

The `sessions.patch` call no longer captures its return value:

```typescript
// Before (main): return value silently discarded
await client.request("sessions.patch", { key, model: nextModel || null });

// After (PR): same RPC call, but refresh + reconcile follows
await client.request("sessions.patch", {
  key: targetSessionKey,
  model: nextModel || null,
});

// Read the authoritative model from the refreshed sessions list,
// not from patched.resolved. The session row carries the final
// effective model after all server-side fallback lifecycle hooks.
await refreshSessionOptions(state);
if (nextModel) {
  const row = state.sessionsResult?.sessions?.find(
    (s) => s.key === targetSessionKey,
  );
  const resolvedValue = resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue(
    row?.model, row?.modelProvider,
    state.chatModelCatalog ?? [],
  );
  const resolvedOverride = createChatModelOverride(resolvedValue);
  if (JSON.stringify(resolvedOverride) !==
      JSON.stringify(state.chatModelOverrides[targetSessionKey])) {
    state.chatModelOverrides = {
      ...state.chatModelOverrides,
      [targetSessionKey]: resolvedOverride,
    };
  }
}
```

The reconciliation runs only for explicit model selections (`if (nextModel)` guard). For Default (`nextModel = ""`), the immediate write set the cache to `null` and reconciliation is skipped — the selector keeps following the default model.

## Design decisions

**Why read from the refreshed sessions list instead of `patched.resolved`?** `patched.resolved` in the `sessions.patch` response represents the request-time model resolution. The sessions list refreshed via `refreshSessionOptions` → `loadSessions` carries the final effective model after all server-side fallback lifecycle hooks have run. This matches the contract already used by the `/model` slash command path (`slash-command-executor.ts:213`), which also reconciled from session-level model data. Consuming the same data source ensures behavioral consistency between the dropdown and the slash command.

**Why reorder `refreshSessionOptions` to before reconciliation?** In the original PR, `refreshSessionOptions` was called after reconciliation — the reconciled cache was written, then immediately followed by a full sessions list refresh that might carry additional corrections. Moving the refresh before reconciliation means the session row data is guaranteed current and authoritative at the point of consumption. The tools refresh (`refreshVisibleToolsEffectiveForCurrentSessionLazy`) is also moved after reconciliation so the tools panel sees the reconciled override.

**Why keep the immediate cache write?** The immediate write (`createChatModelOverride(nextModel)`) happens before the RPC to keep the picker responsive during the round-trip. This is an existing UX pattern used by all `switch*` functions in this file. After the RPC completes and the sessions list is refreshed, the reconciliation block may update the cache with the server-confirmed model. The JSON.stringify guard ensures this second write only happens when there's an actual difference — no unnecessary re-renders.

**Why `JSON.stringify` comparison instead of deep equality?** `ChatModelOverride` is a simple discriminated union with two shapes (`{ kind: "default" }` and `{ kind: "qualified", value: string }`). `JSON.stringify` is sufficient for determining semantic equality and avoids importing a deep-equality utility. The comparison also prevents the cache from being overwritten when the server confirms the exact model the user requested (no fallback).

**Compatibility note**: The reconciliation may change the cached override's `kind` from `"raw"` to `"qualified"` when the server adds a provider prefix. This is benign — all downstream consumers (`normalizeChatModelOverrideValue`, the agents controller, model display helpers) handle both kinds identically and return the same display value. The qualified form is actually more efficient for downstream reads because it skips the catalog ID lookup. Existing caches from before this PR remain untouched until the user explicitly switches models, at which point the cache is reconciled to the new format.

## Risk checklist

**What is the highest-risk area of this change?** The `if (nextModel)` guard — if the dropdown somehow passes a truthy value for the Default selection, reconciliation would incorrectly cache a concrete override. However, the dropdown's value for Default is consistently `""` (empty string), which is the only falsy string value in JavaScript.

**Risk level**: Low risk — JavaScript truthiness: empty string `""` is the only falsy string value. All other dropdown selections are non-empty qualified model references. The `catch` block restores `prevOverride` on RPC failure, preserving error-recovery semantics. The `JSON.stringify` comparison prevents redundant cache writes. The reconciliation source (refreshed sessions list) is already a shipped contract.

**Mitigation**: Existing unit tests cover `createChatModelOverride("") → null`. The `JSON.stringify` comparison prevents redundant cache writes. Error rollback preserves `prevOverride` in the existing `catch` block (unchanged by this PR). The sessions list read path is identical to the shipped `/model` command contract.

**Merge risk declaration**:
- `session-state` — per-session UI model cache reconciliation. Gated on non-empty `nextModel`, occurs after `refreshSessionOptions` completes successfully, with error rollback via existing `catch` block. The state update is conditional on `JSON.stringify` difference, preventing noisy re-renders. The reconciliation source (refreshed sessions list) is the same contract used by the `/model` command.
- `compatibility` — the dropdown now shows the effective model after server fallback, matching the `/model` command behavior. Existing cache entries with unqualified model names are progressively upgraded to qualified form on next user interaction (switch model). All downstream consumers handle both forms identically.

## Architecture context

The WebChat model dropdown and the `/model` slash command share the same `chatModelOverrides` state cache but historically used different reconciliation strategies:

| Path | File | Reads sessions list? | Server fallback visible? |
|------|------|---------------------|-------------------------|
| `/model` command | `slash-command-executor.ts:218` | ✅ Yes | ✅ |
| Model dropdown (before) | `session-controls.ts` (main) | ❌ No | ❌ |
| Model dropdown (after) | `session-controls.ts` (this PR) | ✅ Yes | ✅ |

This PR brings the dropdown to parity with the slash command. Both paths now consume the same authoritative data source (the sessions list after refresh), use the same helper (`resolvePreferredServerChatModelValue`), and follow the same reconciliation contract. The reconciliation order (patch → refresh → reconcile → tools refresh) ensures downstream consumers like the agent tools panel always see the reconciled override.

## Current review state

**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested. V2 of this PR switches from reading `patched.resolved` to reading the refreshed sessions list row, addressing the ClawSweeper suggestion to "reconcile from actual refreshed session/runtime fallback state." All 32 existing tests pass with zero regressions. Real browser screenshots confirm the WebChat UI renders correctly.

target.title: fix(session-delivery): propagate non-ENOENT retry persistence failures
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

In `drainQueuedEntry` (`src/infra/session-delivery-queue-recovery.ts`), the retry
path swallows a non-ENOENT failure when persisting the failed-attempt state:

```ts
try {
  await failSessionDelivery(entry.id, errMsg, opts.stateDir);
  return "failed";
} catch (failErr) {
  if (getErrnoCode(failErr) === "ENOENT") {
    return "already-gone";
  }
  return "failed"; // <- non-ENOENT persistence failure swallowed
}
```

`failSessionDelivery` is what advances the entry's `retryCount` and stamps
`lastAttemptAt` (`session-delivery-queue-storage.ts`). If that write throws for a
reason other than ENOENT (e.g. an IO/`ENOSPC`/`EACCES`/lock error), the code
still returns a clean `"failed"`, so the retry metadata never advances. On the
next recovery cycle the same entry is re-selected at the same `retryCount`, so it
never progresses toward the `retryCount >= maxRetries` terminal move and the
underlying persistence failure is never surfaced.

## Why This Change Was Made

Both sibling terminal-move paths in the same file already treat a non-ENOENT
persistence error as fatal and re-throw it:

- `drainPendingSessionDeliveries` max-retries branch: `if (getErrnoCode(err) !== "ENOENT") throw err;`
- `recoverPendingSessionDeliveries` max-retries branch: same.

The retry path was the only persistence call that silently absorbed a non-ENOENT
error. This change makes it symmetric: ENOENT still means "entry already gone"
(`already-gone`), and any other persistence failure now propagates instead of
being mislabeled as a normal `"failed"`.

## User Impact

A genuine persistence/IO failure during a delivery retry now surfaces (and aborts
the drain cycle, like the existing terminal-move paths) instead of being hidden
behind a delivery-level "retry failed" log while the entry silently stops
advancing. No change for the normal case (delivery fails, fail-state persists) or
for the ENOENT "already removed" case.

## Evidence

- New regression test `propagates a non-ENOENT failure-state persistence error
  instead of swallowing it` in `session-delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`: forces
  `failSessionDelivery` to reject with an `ENOSPC` error during a retry and
  asserts `recoverPendingSessionDeliveries` rejects (and leaves the entry pending)
  rather than resolving with `{ failed: 1 }`.
  - Verified fail-on-main: with the source fix reverted the test fails with
    `promise resolved "{ recovered: 0, failed: 1 }" instead of rejecting`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/session-delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts` -> 7/7 pass.
- `oxlint --type-aware` clean on both files; `oxfmt` clean; `tsgo` core-test lane clean.


## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A non-ENOENT failure while persisting the failed-attempt state during a delivery retry (in `drainQueuedEntry`) is now propagated instead of being mislabeled as a normal `"failed"`, so the entry no longer keeps being re-driven at an unchanged `retryCount`.

Real environment tested: The real `recoverPendingSessionDeliveries` recovery path against an on-disk session-delivery queue under the Vitest infra runtime (`test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts`), exercising the actual patched `src/infra/session-delivery-queue-recovery.ts` module (not a mirror script). `failSessionDelivery` is forced to reject with `ENOSPC` mid-retry via a spread-actual mock that overrides only the storage write fn.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/session-delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 7 passed (7)`. The new case `propagates a non-ENOENT failure-state persistence error instead of swallowing it` asserts `recoverPendingSessionDeliveries` rejects (entry left pending) instead of resolving `{recovered:0, failed:1}`.

Observed result after fix: the injected `ENOSPC` propagates and the recovery cycle aborts with the entry still pending for the next cycle, matching the two sibling terminal-move paths (`drainPendingSessionDeliveries` and `recoverPendingSessionDeliveries` max-retries branches) that already re-throw non-ENOENT. Reverting the source one-liner makes the same test fail with "promise resolved {recovered:0,failed:1} instead of rejecting" (fail-on-main verified).

What was not tested: a live production filesystem actually returning ENOSPC/EACCES on the failed-state write was not exercised; the error is injected at the storage boundary. The propagation contract and the ENOENT "already-gone" branch are both covered by the regression test.

target.title: fix(memory-core): stop leaking per-agent XDG_CONFIG_HOME to mcporter child spawns
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #79847: qmd-manager leaks per-agent XDG_CONFIG_HOME to mcporter child spawns. mcporter >= 0.10 honors XDG_CONFIG_HOME for config discovery, so passing the agent-scoped qmd xdg-config dir causes mcporter to look for mcporter.json under the per-agent path instead of the user-level path. Result: memory search fails with "Unknown MCP server 'qmd'".

## Why This Change Was Made

The QmdMemoryManager constructor built a single this.env object with per-agent XDG overrides and passed it to both runQmd() and runMcporter(). The XDG overrides are correct for qmd (per-agent index isolation) but XDG_CONFIG_HOME breaks mcporter config discovery.

**Fix:** Split into two env objects:
- **this.env** (unchanged) — with XDG_CONFIG_HOME, QMD_CONFIG_DIR, XDG_CACHE_HOME used only for runQmd()
- **this.mcporterEnv** (new) — with QMD_CONFIG_DIR and XDG_CACHE_HOME (preserved for mcporter-launched local qmd MCP servers) but WITHOUT XDG_CONFIG_HOME (so mcporter resolves its own config from user-level paths)

## Evidence

### Live E2E verification with mcporter 0.12.2

**BUG reproduction — XDG_CONFIG_HOME points to per-agent empty dir:**


**FIX verification — XDG_CONFIG_HOME not set:**


### Build
- pnpm build -> exit 0

### Tests
- pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager -> all passed
- Updated regression test: verifies XDG_CONFIG_HOME is undefined for mcporter spawns while QMD_CONFIG_DIR and XDG_CACHE_HOME are preserved

### Diff scope
- 2 files changed, +25/-5 lines (only qmd-manager.ts and its test)

## User Impact

Users with memory.backend: qmd + mcporter.enabled: true + mcporter >= 0.10 can use memory search. Previously locked to mcporter <= 0.9.x.

target.title: fix(agents): enable bundled static catalog fallback for cron Attempt 2
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Plugin-provided models (e.g. opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash) in the bundled static catalog are now resolvable by non-plugin-harness cron sessions via the embedded runner's Attempt 2 model resolution path.

- **Problem**: Non-plugin-harness isolated cron sessions fail with "Unknown model" for plugin-provided models, even though the models exist in the bundled static catalog. Attempt 2 `resolveModelAsync` is called without `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback`, so the static catalog is never consulted.
- **Solution**: Pass `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback: true` in the Attempt 2 `resolveModelAsync` call, enabling the static catalog fallback path for models that agent discovery cannot resolve.
- **What changed**: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts` — 5 lines added.
- **What did NOT change**: Attempt 1 behavior (still `skipAgentDiscovery: true`, `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` still gated on `pluginHarnessOwnsTransport`); Attempt 2 structure; model discovery ordering.
- Fixes #95500

## Evidence

### Root cause

The embedded runner's two-attempt model resolution in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts:1070-1125`:

1. **Attempt 1** (line 1074-1096): `skipAgentDiscovery: true`, `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` only set when `pluginHarnessOwnsTransport === true` (cron sessions: `false`)
2. If Attempt 1 fails, `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` is called (line 1108)
3. **Attempt 2** (line 1111-1123): `resolveModelAsync` called with only `{ workspaceDir, authProfileId }` — **no** `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback`

The bundled static catalog already contains opencode-go plugin models (`extensions/opencode-go/openclaw.plugin.json:49`), but Attempt 2's resolver never checks it because the flag is missing.

**Provenance**: `joshavant` added `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` in `da4671ebccfb`. Attempt 1 gates it on `pluginHarnessOwnsTransport`, but Attempt 2 never sets it. At the time the flag was introduced, non-plugin-harness paths were not expected to need static catalog lookup. The isolated cron executor now routes plugin-provided models through the embedded runner, exposing this gap.

**Confidence**: `clear` — source inspection confirms a single missing boolean flag at a single call site.

### Real behavior proof

The proof exercises the exact `resolveModelAsync` production function with `skipProviderRuntimeHooks: true` to simulate the isolated cron environment (where plugins are not loaded, so dynamic model hooks are unavailable). The ONLY variable changed across scenarios is `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback`.

**Environment**: Linux x86_64, Node v22.11.0, branch `fix/static-catalog-fallback-95500`

```console
$ node --import tsx proof-96070-v2.ts
====================================================================
  REAL BEHAVIOR PROOF v2 — PR #96070
  fix(agents): enable bundled static catalog fallback for cron Attempt 2
  Platform: linux Node: v22.11.0
====================================================================

  Isolation: skipProviderRuntimeHooks: true (simulates cron — no plugin hooks)

── A) Attempt 1: skipAgentDiscovery=true, no static catalog fallback ──
    resolved: model=null
    verdict: ❌ Unknown model (expected)

── B) Attempt 2: NO allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback (current main bug) ──
    resolved: model=null
    verdict: ❌ Unknown model (expected — reproduces bug)

── C) Attempt 2: allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback=true (THIS PR's fix) ──
    resolved: model=found
    verdict: ✅ model resolved

====================================================================
  RESULT MATRIX
====================================================================

  | Scenario                                      | Result                    |
  |-----------------------------------------------|--------------------------|
  | A) Attempt 1 style: skip discovery, no SC      | ❌ Unknown model         |
  | B) Attempt 2: no static catalog (main bug)     | ❌ Unknown model         |
  | C) Attempt 2: allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback| ✅ model resolved        |

  The proof is valid only if B fails AND C succeeds:
  B ❌ + C ✅ = ✅ PROOF VALID — flag is the decisive factor
```

### Source code comparison — main vs this PR

| Call site | Current main | This PR |
|---|---|---|
| Attempt 1 `skipAgentDiscovery` | `true` | unchanged |
| Attempt 1 `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` | `pluginHarnessOwnsTransport` | unchanged |
| Attempt 2 `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` | (not set — static catalog never consulted) | **`true`** ← the fix |
| `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` between attempts | called | unchanged |

### Test results


target.title: fix(qa-lab): add live turn timeout env override
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

QA Lab live-frontier runs sometimes need a longer per-turn timeout than the built-in model-family floors allow, especially when validating slow frontier/provider routes or shared-runner conditions. Today operators can tune several QA Lab and transport timeouts with `OPENCLAW_QA_*_TIMEOUT_MS` env vars, but the live-frontier agent turn timeout itself is not adjustable without changing code.

This is intentionally separate from #96567. That PR fixes the `claude-cli/*` timeout bucket classification. This PR adds the operator escape hatch for slow live-frontier runs.

## Why This Change Was Made

The existing `resolveQaLiveTurnTimeoutMs` path already receives a caller fallback and then lets the provider policy enforce model-specific floors. This change adds `OPENCLAW_QA_LIVE_TURN_TIMEOUT_MS` at that fallback boundary instead of bypassing provider policy.

That means:

- A higher env value raises the floor for live-frontier turns.
- A lower env value does not accidentally undercut existing provider-specific floors like GPT-5 or Claude Opus.
- Invalid env values fall back to the existing timeout behavior.

## User Impact

QA Lab operators can run slow live-frontier scenarios with:

```bash
OPENCLAW_QA_LIVE_TURN_TIMEOUT_MS=420000 pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode live-frontier ...
```

Existing runs without the env var are unchanged. Mock lanes keep using the caller fallback unless the env value raises that fallback.

## Evidence

Test Plan (run from repo root):

- `pnpm test extensions/qa-lab/src/live-timeout.test.ts` - PASS, 1 file / 8 tests.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` - PASS.
- `pnpm format:docs:check docs/help/testing.md` - PASS, docs formatting clean.
- `git diff --check` - PASS.
- `~/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/openclaw-pre-pr.sh --repo /root/projects/openclaw --plan-only --no-rebase` - PASS; mapped scope to `extensions`, `extensionTests`, and `docs`.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` - PASS.

Known local tooling gap:

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/qa-lab/src/live-timeout.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-timeout.ts` was attempted twice, but the local wrapper stayed in `prepare-extension-package-boundary-artifacts.mjs` with no lint diagnostics; the attempts were stopped so no local check process remained. CI remains the canonical oxlint proof for this lane.

🤖 AI-assisted with GPT-5 Codex.

target.title: feat: configurable default agent ID (escape the 'main' prison) 🦞
target.body:
## Summary

Adds support for overriding the hardcoded `"main"` fallback agent ID, so users with a single custom-named agent no longer get a ghost `agents/main/` default path/session identity when no `agents.list` is configured.

## The Problem

`DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` falls back to `"main"`. Some resolution paths run from partial config or before a full multi-agent list exists, so a setup that intends a custom default agent can still resolve the main session key and default agent directory as `main`.

## Solution

Two complementary override mechanisms:

### 1. Config key: `agents.defaultAgentId`

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaultAgentId": "maine-lobster"
  }
}
```

### 2. Env var: `OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID`

```bash
export OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID=maine-lobster
```

### Priority (highest → lowest)

1. `agents.list[].default: true` (explicit default agent)
2. `agents.list[0]` (first configured agent)
3. `agents.defaultAgentId` config key
4. `OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` env var
5. Hardcoded `"main"` (backward compatible)

## Changes

- Adds `agents.defaultAgentId` to the config schema/types and generated docs baseline.
- Updates `resolveDefaultAgentId()`, `listAgentIds()`, `resolveDefaultAgentDir()`, and main-session key resolution to honor config/env fallback values.
- Keeps populated `agents.list` behavior unchanged: list default/first entry still wins.
- Updates provider auth/default-agent directory resolution to use the effective default agent.
- Adds regression tests for config default, env fallback, default dir, and main-session key behavior.

## Testing

- `pnpm config:schema:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test src/agents/agent-scope-config.test.ts src/config/sessions/main-session.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-key.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD origin/main` → merge-clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A config-only default agent ID should stop OpenClaw from falling back to the hardcoded `main` agent for the default agent directory and main session key.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout rebased onto latest `origin/main`, running the patched TypeScript source directly with Node/tsx in `/root/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-pr-acpx/pr-51762`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { resolveDefaultAgentId, resolveDefaultAgentDir } from './src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts';
import { resolveMainSessionKey } from './src/config/sessions/main-session.ts';
const cfg = { agents: { defaultAgentId: 'Ops Team' }, session: { mainKey: 'main' } };
const env = { OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR: '/tmp/openclaw-real-proof' };
console.log('defaultAgentId=' + resolveDefaultAgentId(cfg, env));
console.log('defaultAgentDir=' + resolveDefaultAgentDir(cfg, env));
console.log('mainSessionKey=' + resolveMainSessionKey(cfg, env));
EOF
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output copied from the patched real source command above:

```console
defaultAgentId=ops-team
defaultAgentDir=/tmp/openclaw-real-proof/agents/ops-team/agent
mainSessionKey=agent:ops-team:main
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `agents.defaultAgentId: "Ops Team"` is normalized to `ops-team`, the default agent directory resolves under `/tmp/openclaw-real-proof/agents/ops-team/agent`, and the main session key resolves to `agent:ops-team:main` instead of `main`.
- **What was not tested**: No browser/UI flow was tested; this PR changes config/session/agent resolution behavior and was verified through the live runtime resolver functions plus the targeted regression/check suite above.

Fixes #17477 🦞

target.title: fix(node-host): clarify unusable node exec cwd failures
target.body:
# PR draft — node exec: report an unusable working directory instead of blaming the shell

## What Problem This Solves

When `exec host=node` runs a command and the forwarded working directory does not
exist on the node host, the node host surfaces a misleading error:

```
spawn /bin/sh ENOENT
```

`/bin/sh` is present — the real cause is the missing `cwd`. Node/libuv performs the
pre-exec `chdir(cwd)` and, when it fails, reports the failure against the command
(`argv[0]`) rather than the directory. This exact misattribution drove issue #85202
and three revisions of the closed PR #85543, all of which chased a non-existent
"missing shell" problem (shell fallback) that could never fix a missing-directory
error.

The omitted-cwd case (the headline #85202 reproduction) is already fixed on `main`
by #58977 — the gateway no longer forwards its own `process.cwd()` to remote node
exec. The residual gap is the **non-approval node-exec path** (`security=full` /
`ask=off`, the headless-node default): an *explicit* forwarded workdir that is
absent on the node still reaches `spawn()` unvalidated and yields the misleading
`spawn <shell> ENOENT`. The approval path already fails closed with a clear message
(`approval requires an existing canonical cwd`); the non-approval path does not.

## Why This Change Was Made

This is a **diagnostic-only** change: the supplied `cwd` is never dropped, rewritten,
or replaced with the node default — only the error *text* changes, and only when the
spawn failed because the `cwd` is not a usable directory.

There are two distinct spawn failure modes for a bad `cwd`, and Node surfaces them
differently:
- **Missing directory** → libuv fails the pre-exec `chdir` and emits an asynchronous
  `error` event carrying `spawn <argv0> ENOENT`. The run already *failed closed*
  (`success=false`); only the message was misleading. The fix rewrites that message.
- **`cwd` exists but is a file** → `spawn()` throws `ENOTDIR` **synchronously** rather
  than emitting `error`. Because the node host dispatches `handleInvoke` with `void`
  (`src/node-host/runner.ts`), that throw escaped as an unhandled rejection, which the
  global handler (`src/index.ts`) converts into `process.exit(1)` — crashing the node
  host instead of failing closed and never sending a result. The fix wraps `spawn()` so
  this case is routed through the same fail-closed result and clarified message.

Deliberately **not** done: dropping a missing/explicit cwd and falling back to the
node's default directory. That was the rejected shape of the prior PR #85543 and
would silently run a command in the wrong place — a fail-closed regression.

Design notes:
- The clarification lives in the node host (`runCommand`), where the cwd is actually
  interpreted. The gateway cannot stat a remote node's filesystem, so this must be
  node-side.
- Zero hot-path cost: the `statSync` runs only on a spawn **failure** (the async
  `error` handler or the synchronous `spawn()` catch), after the command has already
  failed, honoring the "no freshness polling on hot paths" rule.
- The predicate leaves genuine missing-executable errors untouched: if the `cwd`
  exists and is a directory, an `ENOENT` is about `argv[0]`, and the original
  message is preserved.

## User Impact

Operators running `exec host=node` against nodes whose filesystem layout differs
from the gateway (e.g. Docker gateway + systemd Linux nodes, cross-platform) now get
an actionable error:

```
node exec working directory does not exist on the node host: /gateway/only/path (os reported: spawn /bin/sh ENOENT)
```

instead of a message that points at the shell. And when the forwarded path resolves to
a *file* on the node, the run now fails closed with
`node exec working directory is not a directory on the node host: …` instead of
crashing the node-host process on an unhandled `ENOTDIR`. No behavior, config, or
compatibility change; the command still fails closed and the `cwd` is never dropped.

## Evidence

- Unit coverage in `src/node-host/invoke.test.ts` for `clarifyNodeExecCwdSpawnError`:
  missing cwd → clarified; cwd-is-a-file → "is not a directory"; existing cwd (genuine
  missing executable) → message preserved; no cwd → preserved; unrelated error code →
  preserved.
- Behavioral coverage for `runCommand` (real spawn): a missing cwd (async `ENOENT`) and
  a cwd that is a file (synchronous `ENOTDIR` throw) both return `success=false` with the
  clarified message instead of rejecting the promise / crashing the node host.
- Reproduced the libuv behavior locally on Node 22.22.2: a missing cwd emits an async
  `error` (`code=ENOENT message="spawn /bin/sh ENOENT"` — the command, not the dir),
  while a cwd that is a file makes `spawn()` throw **synchronously**
  (`code=ENOTDIR message="spawn ENOTDIR" syscall="spawn"`), bypassing the `error` event.
- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=… node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/node-host/invoke.test.ts`
  → 11 passed; 1 pre-existing, environment-specific failure unrelated to this change
  (the `/bin/echo` allow-always coverage test, which fails identically on clean `main`
  in this sandbox).
- `oxfmt --check` + `oxlint` on the touched files → clean.
- `tsgo:core` (prod, includes `src/node-host/invoke.ts`) and `tsgo:core:test`
  (includes `src/node-host/invoke.test.ts`) → both clean.

Follow-up (sibling surface): the macOS app exec host (`runViaMacAppExecHost`) spawns on
a different machine and would need the same clarification in the companion app to cover
that transport; out of scope here and flagged for a separate change.

Fixes the diagnostic half of #85202 (the omitted-cwd half is already fixed by #58977).

target.title: fix(anthropic): handle max_turns stop reason and surface retry-limit details to users
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep Anthropic `max_turns` normalized to the existing public `length` stop reason in both Anthropic stream paths.
- Preserve the raw provider stop reason in assistant diagnostics as `provider_stop_reason`, so downstream code can distinguish Anthropic `max_turns` from ordinary `max_tokens` truncation without expanding the public `StopReason` union.
- Surface a specific incomplete-turn warning for Anthropic `max_turns` terminal turns instead of the generic “couldn't generate a response” fallback.
- Keep retry-limit internal breaker diagnostics in `meta.error` while using a shorter user-visible message for repeated idle-timeout exhaustion.

## Linked context

Closes #78145

## Root cause and scope

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix at the Anthropic stream normalization boundary plus the runner user-message boundary that consumes the normalized terminal turn.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High. The final committed diff has focused RED/GREEN coverage for both Anthropic stream paths, incomplete-turn messaging, and retry-limit payload formatting; ClawSweeper commit review found nothing.
- Root cause: Anthropic can report a provider turn-limit terminal condition as `stop_reason: "max_turns"`. Mapping that raw provider reason only to public `length` preserves the existing contract, but `length` alone is not enough context for runner/session finalization to tell provider turn exhaustion apart from ordinary output-token exhaustion.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The raw provider stop reason is captured where Anthropic stream events are normalized, before runner finalization consumes the assistant turn. The runner can then keep the public `length` semantics while using diagnostics to show the user actionable Anthropic max-turns context. This fixes the source invariant instead of adding a downstream string check after the provider-specific reason has been lost.
- Patch quality notes: The change is deliberately split between provider/transport normalization, incomplete-turn user messaging, and retry-limit payload formatting. The test-only type cast builds a fixture for an existing persisted assistant-message shape; production code reads diagnostics defensively. The new helper returns `false` for malformed diagnostics so old assistant records and non-Anthropic turns continue to use the existing generic fallback. The retry-limit change does not alter retry counts or timeout policy; it separates public payload text from internal `meta.error.message`.
- Why it matters / User impact: When Anthropic stops because the provider max-turns limit was reached, users see a specific instruction to start a fresh session or retry a smaller task instead of a generic incomplete-response warning. Internal retry-limit breaker details such as cost-runaway wording and issue IDs no longer leak into user-visible payload text.
- What did NOT change: This PR does not add a new public stop reason, does not classify `max_turns` as `error`, does not rotate/fail over models for `max_turns`, and does not change config, schema, credentials, routing, timeout thresholds, retry counts, or non-Anthropic providers.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The canonical public terminal-state contract stays `StopReason: "length"` for provider/output budget termination. Raw provider specificity is carried in extensible assistant diagnostics, matching the existing diagnostic pattern used for Anthropic refusal metadata.
- Related open PR / same-contract scan: This update is for the existing open PR #94130 linked to #78145, not a competing replacement PR. The same-contract risk is confined to Anthropic Messages stop-reason normalization and assistant diagnostics; no separate public `StopReason` contract PR is being introduced here.
- Out of scope: Live hosted Anthropic budget exhaustion orchestration, public stop-reason expansion, session migration, config/schema changes, timeout threshold changes, retry/failover policy changes, credentials/routing changes, and non-Anthropic providers.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Anthropic `max_turns` responses need to remain public `length` terminal turns while preserving enough raw provider context to show actionable user guidance.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with Node v24.16.0 and the repository package environment. The runtime proof directly invoked OpenClaw's production `streamAnthropic` function against an Anthropic-format SSE `Response`, so it exercised the real provider stream parser and stop-reason mapper without making a live API call.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  pnpm exec tsx --eval '<script imports streamAnthropic, feeds stop_reason=max_turns, prints normalized_stop_reason, and exits nonzero unless the result is length>'
  ```
- Evidence after fix: Console output from the runtime stream proof:
  ```text
  Anthropic max_turns runtime stream proof
  raw_stop_reason=max_turns
  normalized_stop_reason=length
  content=[{"type":"text","text":"Partial turn"}]
  usage={"input":4,"output":8,"cacheRead":0,"cacheWrite":0,"totalTokens":12,"cost":{"input":0,"output":0,"cacheRead":0,"cacheWrite":0,"total":0}}
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The production Anthropic provider stream path consumed a `message_delta` with `stop_reason: "max_turns"`, preserved partial assistant text and token usage, and returned `normalized_stop_reason=length`. The new regression coverage additionally asserts that both Anthropic paths now attach the raw `max_turns` diagnostic and that the runner surfaces the max-turns-specific user message.
- What was not tested: A live hosted Anthropic API run that actually exhausts the upstream turn budget, because forcing `max_turns` from the hosted API on demand is not deterministic.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The issue-specific runtime proof replays an Anthropic-format SSE response through OpenClaw's real provider stream code instead of waiting for a hosted Anthropic API budget exhaustion event.
- Before evidence:
  ```text
  src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts: expected result.diagnostics to contain provider_stop_reason, received undefined
  src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts: expected result.diagnostics to contain provider_stop_reason, received undefined
  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts: expected Anthropic max-turns warning, received generic incomplete-response warning
  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/retry-limit.test.ts: expected public idle-timeout message, received internal cost-runaway breaker details
  ```

## Tests and validation

- Target test file: `src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts`; `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts`; `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`; `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/retry-limit.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: Anthropic emits `message_delta.delta.stop_reason: "max_turns"` after partial text and usage. OpenClaw returns `stopReason: "length"`, preserves content and usage, and attaches a `provider_stop_reason` diagnostic containing `rawStopReason: "max_turns"` and `normalizedStopReason: "length"` in both Anthropic stream implementations.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The tests cover the two stream-normalization boundaries, the incomplete-turn user-message consumer, and the retry-limit formatting boundary without widening public API shape.

Focused RED/GREEN regression command after the final commit:
```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/retry-limit.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts -t "maps Anthropic max_turns|keeps internal idle-timeout|surfaces Anthropic max_turns"
```

Focused RED/GREEN result:
```text
Test Files  6 passed (6)
     Tests  6 passed | 415 skipped (421)
```

Related file coverage command after the final commit:
```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/retry-limit.test.ts
```

Related file coverage result:
```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
     Tests  197 passed (197)
```

Regression coverage added:
- `src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts` covers Anthropic `message_delta.delta.stop_reason: "max_turns"` in the provider path and asserts `provider_stop_reason` diagnostics.
- `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts` covers the same stop reason in the transport-stream path and asserts the same diagnostic shape.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts` covers the max-turns-specific incomplete-turn user message.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/retry-limit.test.ts` covers public/internal message separation for repeated idle-timeout exhaustion.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`) Anthropic max-turns terminal turns now surface a specific recovery instruction, and repeated idle-timeout retry exhaustion no longer exposes internal breaker details in the visible payload.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: session-state` and provider/runner terminal-state behavior.
- Risk explanation: Stop-reason classification and terminal-turn messaging can affect how incomplete Anthropic runs are explained to users.
- Why acceptable: The public normalized stop reason remains the existing `length` value; no public union value or wire contract is expanded. Raw `max_turns` context is carried in assistant diagnostics, which are already extensible. Regression tests cover both Anthropic stream paths, content/usage preservation, runner user-message resolution, and retry-limit payload formatting.

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Ready for maintainer review after this update lands on the PR branch and remote checks rerun.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?
- No known author-side code or local validation action remains. A live hosted Anthropic `max_turns` exhaustion event was not forced because it is not deterministic on demand; the runtime proof uses a provider-shaped SSE response through OpenClaw's production stream code.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
- RF-001: `max_turns` no longer relies on public `length` alone; raw provider context is preserved before runner/session finalization consumes the terminal turn.
- RF-002: The PR treats `max_turns` as defensive raw provider stream handling without adding a new public Anthropic SDK or OpenClaw `StopReason` contract value.
- RF-003: Runtime proof and RED/GREEN regression evidence cover the provider-shaped stream behavior, diagnostic preservation, incomplete-turn user message, and retry-limit user-visible payload behavior.

target.title: fix(browser): move ssrf-runtime-internal exports to public ssrf-runti…
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
closes #96639
Fixes the browser plugin lazy-load crash where `openclaw browser status` and any browser agent tool fail with:

```
Package subpath './plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal' is not defined by "exports"
in openclaw/package.json imported from openclaw\dist\cdp.helpers-B5T7VOSu.js
```

## Why This Change Was Made

`registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass` and `fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard` were only exported from the private `openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal` subpath. Export both functions from the public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime` path and update consumer imports.

## What Changed

9 files, +10/−15 lines. No `package.json` or config changes.

## Evidence

Environment: Linux x64, Node v22.19.0, Chromium 148.

### Before (origin/main) — confirmed broken

```text
# dist file excluded from package
$ ls dist/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal.js
FILE NOT FOUND

# Node.js exports map rejects the import
$ node -e "import('openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal')"
Error: Package subpath './plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal' is not defined by "exports"

# Two functions NOT in public ssrf-runtime
$ node --import tsx -e "
const mod = await import('openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime');
console.log('registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass:', typeof mod.registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass);
console.log('fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard:', typeof mod.fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard);
"
registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass: undefined
fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard: undefined
```

### After (fix/browser-ssrf-runtime-import) — confirmed fixed

```text
# Both functions importable from public ssrf-runtime
$ node --import tsx -e "
const mod = await import('openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime');
console.log('registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass:', typeof mod.registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass);
console.log('fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard:', typeof mod.fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard);
"
registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass: function
fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard: function

# E2E: browser plugin lazy-loads, gateway returns full status
$ pnpm build && pnpm openclaw gateway start && pnpm openclaw browser status
profile:          openclaw
enabled:          true
transport:        cdp
cdpUrl:           http://127.0.0.1:18800
detectedBrowser:  chrome
detectedPath:     /usr/bin/google-chrome-stable
EXIT: 0

# Private subpath infrastructure unchanged
$ grep "!dist/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal" package.json
"!dist/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal.js"
```

### Unit tests

| Test | Result |
|------|--------|
| ollama embedding-provider (25) | 25/25 passed |
| browser cdp-proxy-bypass (28) | 27/28 (1 pre-existing env issue) |
| contract guardrail (21) | 21/21 passed |
| surface-report --check | exit 0 |

### Test conclusion

| Check | Before (origin/main) | After (fix) |
|-------|---------------------|-------------|
| Public import of both functions | `undefined` | `function` |
| `openclaw browser status` | — | exit 0, full output |
| Unit tests | — | 73/74 passed |
| Private dist exclusion | unchanged | unchanged |

## Root Cause

`registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass` (`a37ebb2d49`) and `fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard` (`fd2a9adbe6`) were placed in the private `ssrf-runtime-internal` module. Its dist artifact was excluded via `!dist/` (added by `2229122077`) and it had no `package.json` exports entry. Node.js native module resolution rejected the import at runtime.
