target.title: feat: add Pioneer.ai as an inference provider with live model discovery
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw had no support for Pioneer.ai as an inference provider. Users with Pioneer.ai accounts could not use it as a model source, and there was no way to browse or select Pioneer models from the UI model picker.

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR adds Pioneer.ai as a first-class OpenClaw inference provider with live model discovery:

- **Pioneer plugin** (`extensions/pioneer/`) registers Pioneer.ai as an OpenAI-compatible provider at `https://api.pioneer.ai/v1`. Authentication uses a `PIONEER_API_KEY` env var or auth profile.
- **Live model discovery via `augmentModelCatalog`** — The `augmentModelCatalog` hook calls the Pioneer `/v1/models` endpoint (returns `{ data: [...] }` — standard OpenAI-compatible shape parsed by the shared `getCachedLiveProviderModelRows` helper) when a Pioneer API key is configured. This is the hook that the gateway's `loadModelCatalog` → `augmentModelCatalogWithProviderPlugins` path calls, which is what feeds the UI model picker. The `catalog.run` hook (`buildPioneerCatalogResult`) is used by the CLI `models list --provider pioneer` path.
- **60-second TTL model cache** — `getCachedLiveProviderModelRows` caches live model rows for 60 seconds per provider. New models appear within one cache TTL without a gateway restart.
- **Gateway catalog warm** — A startup sidecar pre-warms the gateway model catalog on startup (read-only then full-discovery, no recurring loop) so the full Pioneer model list is available in the UI picker from the first open.
- **Provider wildcard allowlist** — Onboarding sets `pioneer/*` in `agents.defaults.models` so all live-discovered Pioneer models are visible in the picker without enumerating them individually.
- **Default model** — `pioneer/auto` is set as the default primary at onboard time, routing each request to the best available Pioneer model automatically.
- **`normalizeResolvedModel` hook** — Rewrites bare `auto` → `pioneer/auto` at transport time so the Pioneer API receives the correct routing alias.

## User Impact

- Users can now add Pioneer.ai as an inference provider and use any of its 78+ models.
- The UI model picker shows the full live Pioneer model list after gateway startup.
- New Pioneer models appear automatically within the 60s model cache TTL — no gateway restart needed.
- `pioneer/auto` as the default model means users get the best Pioneer model without manually selecting one.

## Evidence

**Live API contract** — Pioneer `/v1/models` returns `{ data: [...], first_id, last_id, has_more, object, models }` with standard OpenAI list pagination. The `data` key contains model rows with `id`, `display_name`, `max_input_tokens`, `max_tokens`, `capabilities`, etc. The shared `getCachedLiveProviderModelRows` helper parses `data` arrays by default, so no custom `readRows` override is needed.

**`pnpm openclaw models list --provider pioneer`** (auth key redacted):
```
Model                                      Input      Ctx         Local Auth  Tags
pioneer/anthropic/pioneer-auto             text       1024k       no    yes
pioneer/auto                               text       1024k       no    yes   default
pioneer/claude-3-7-sonnet-latest           text       8k          no    yes
pioneer/claude-haiku-4-5                   text       195k        no    yes
pioneer/claude-opus-4-1                    text       195k        no    yes
pioneer/claude-opus-4-5                    text       195k        no    yes
pioneer/claude-opus-4-6                    text       977k        no    yes
pioneer/claude-opus-4-8                    text       977k        no    yes
pioneer/claude-sonnet-4-6                  text       977k        no    yes
pioneer/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.1          text       160k        no    yes
pioneer/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash      text       977k        no    yes
pioneer/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro        text       977k        no    yes
pioneer/gemini-3-flash                     text       1024k       no    yes
pioneer/gemini-3.5-flash                   text       1024k       no    yes
... (78 models total via live discovery)
```

**Gateway UI catalog** — SQLite `agent_model_catalogs` shows 21KB cached entries (full live catalog) vs the previous 2-model static result (confirmed by cache size and model count check via CLI).

**Tests:**
- `extensions/pioneer/provider-catalog.test.ts` — 9 tests covering live catalog building, static-only model injection, ID normalization, fallback behavior
- `extensions/pioneer/index.test.ts` — 5 tests covering plugin registration, onboard config, and `normalizeResolvedModel` (auto → pioneer/auto transport rewrite)
- `src/commands/models/list.provider-catalog.test.ts` — 25 tests pass (unchanged)

target.title: fix(codex): wait for turn completion after post-tool handoff
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes premature Codex Harness turn completion after a tool handoff.

## What Problem This Solves

After a Codex turn has crossed a tool handoff, an assistant `item/completed` notification is not a reliable final-answer boundary. The model may still be continuing the same Codex turn, and the correct release signal is `turn/completed`.

This was an item-level versus turn-level lifecycle mismatch in the native Codex app-server runtime plugin:

- Before tool handoff, a completed assistant item can release the turn.
- After tool handoff, completed assistant items can be intermediate progress/narration while the same turn continues.
- Codex ACP does not hit the same failure mode because its collector finishes on the turn-level completion boundary.

## Changes

- `isAssistantCompletionReleaseNotification(...)` now returns false once the turn has crossed a tool handoff.
- Existing post-tool wait behavior is updated to complete only after `turn/completed`.
- Adds one regression test for the failing lifecycle: post-tool assistant item completion without `turn/completed` must not release the run.

The PR is intentionally limited to 2 files and 1 commit.

## Evidence

Current PR head: `cdaeef342d6e4a5b9301b9d8d433353416567d7a`.

```text
cd /tmp/openclaw-pr97153
pnpm test -- --run extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       65 passed (65)
```

The current-head regression coverage includes `does not release post-tool assistant items without turn completion` in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts`. That scenario emits a post-tool assistant item completion and verifies OpenClaw does not resolve the Codex app-server attempt at the assistant-item boundary; it waits for `turn/completed` or the post-tool idle guard instead.

### Real Runtime Proof (2026-06-29)

This proof uses a real Codex Harness attempt through the community Codex app-server runtime path, not the mocked-client proof path.

```text
OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=trace \
ARK_API_KEY=<redacted> \
timeout --kill-after=10s 900s bash command.todo.txt
```

Run identity:

```text
PR head: cdaeef342d6e4a5b9301b9d8d433353416567d7a
runtime: Codex Harness via extensions/codex app-server
model: codex/deepseek-v4-pro
sessionId: 4f480007-dd22-4ed7-be0b-2c4324e8b424
threadId: 019f11a5-0931-72a3-87c4-cea8d8d39427
turnId: 019f11a5-0966-73c1-8ae3-6e084491787e
runId: bd0249cf-5606-4483-b96b-a4da1f72f760
```

Redacted trace excerpt from the real run:

```text
[agent/embedded] [trace:codex-app-server] thread lifecycle: runId=bd0249cf-5606-4483-b96b-a4da1f72f760 sessionId=4f480007-dd22-4ed7-be0b-2c4324e8b424 sessionKey=agent:prompt-replay-a:prompt-replay:a:task_tpl_a_cn_x7dqx0qrlw4j_python_cli_log_inspector:run_001 action=started totalMs=89 stages=...thread-start-request:70ms@86ms...
[diagnostic] session state: sessionId=4f480007-dd22-4ed7-be0b-2c4324e8b424 sessionKey=agent:prompt-replay-a:prompt-replay:a:task_tpl_a_cn_x7dqx0qrlw4j_python_cli_log_inspector:run_001 prev=idle new=processing reason="run_started" queueDepth=0
[diagnostic] run registered: sessionId=4f480007-dd22-4ed7-be0b-2c4324e8b424 totalActive=1
[agent/embedded] codex app-server raw notification received
[agent/embedded] codex app-server raw notification received
...
```

The trajectory for the same real run shows the attempt continuing across many tool result boundaries and finishing only after the terminal model/run completion, not after an intermediate post-tool assistant item:

```text
40 tool.call       2026-06-29T04:38:59.580Z bash
41 tool.result     2026-06-29T04:38:59.584Z completed
42 tool.call       2026-06-29T04:39:06.480Z bash
43 tool.result     2026-06-29T04:39:07.402Z completed
44 model.completed 2026-06-29T04:39:40.777Z
45 session.ended   2026-06-29T04:39:40.781Z success
```

The session transcript for `4f480007-dd22-4ed7-be0b-2c4324e8b424` includes the final assistant response after the last tool result with `stopReason=stop` and `usage.totalTokens=53534`. That confirms the harness did not release at an intermediate post-tool assistant item boundary; it waited until the real turn reached the terminal completion path and then ended successfully.

## Compatibility Note

This is an intentional fail-closed behavior change for malformed or incomplete post-tool Codex app-server streams. After a tool handoff, OpenClaw now waits for the typed `turn/completed` terminal signal or the existing post-tool idle guard instead of treating completed assistant item text as a partial final answer. That prevents early truncation of still-running Codex turns, but maintainers should explicitly accept this boundary change before merge.

target.title: fix(session): defer flushPendingToolResults by one event-loop tick
target.body:
## Summary

Defer `flushPendingToolResults` by one event-loop tick so pending I/O callbacks (e.g. Feishu message-tool HTTP responses) can write real tool results before synthetic errors are injected. Includes a regression test for the multi-tool-call partial-result scenario.

- Problem: `flushPendingToolResultsAfterIdle` calls `flushPendingToolResults()` immediately after the agent reports idle. When a tool call's HTTP response is still in-flight at that moment, the flush injects a synthetic "[openclaw] missing tool result" error. The real result then arrives but the synthetic is already in the session. In Feishu channel, this causes the message tool to show "Something went wrong" (#84134).
- Solution: Add `await new Promise<void>((resolve) => setImmediate(resolve))` before `flushPendingToolResults()` in `flushPendingToolResultsAfterIdle`. This drains one event-loop iteration, allowing pending I/O callbacks to write real tool results and clear the pending map before synthetics are injected.
- What changed: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/wait-for-idle-before-flush.ts` — +7 lines (import + defer); `wait-for-idle-before-flush.test.ts` — +55 lines (3 tests, fails on main); `src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts` — +37 lines (regression test)
- What did NOT change: `guardedAppend` flush check unchanged; `flushPendingToolResults` logic unchanged; aborted-run immediate-flush path (timeoutMs=0) unaffected

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts — Session tool result guard
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #84134 — Feishu message tool "missing tool result" regression
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression
- Related: #84419 (transcript repair dedup mitigation), #84708 (replay poison recovery)

## Motivation

When the Feishu channel's agent calls the `message` tool, `sendMessageFeishu()` makes an HTTP request. While the response is in-flight, the agent may finish producing output. The agent runner cleanup then calls `flushPendingToolResultsAfterIdle` → `flushPendingToolResults()`, which sees the pending `message` tool call and injects a synthetic "[openclaw] missing tool result" error. The real HTTP response then arrives and writes the real result, but the synthetic is already in the session.

Reproduction via manual `guard.flushPendingToolResults()` confirms the race: a synthetic error is injected for pending tool calls before the real result is appended.

The `setImmediate` defer drains one event-loop iteration between the idle check and the flush, allowing I/O callbacks (HTTP responses) to complete first.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: `flushPendingToolResultsAfterIdle` now drains one event-loop tick before flushing, giving in-flight I/O callbacks a chance to write real tool results

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v24.13.1, branch `fix/feishu-message-tool-result-84134-v2` @ commit 72129ee

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Event-loop race reproduction (fails on current main — 2 results, synth=true)
node -e "
function mk() { const p=new Map(), l=[]; return {
  track(id,n) { p.set(id,n); },
  msgs() { return [...l]; },
  flushSync() { for(const [id,n] of p) l.push({role:'tr',toolCallId:id,isError:true}); p.clear(); },
  async flushDef() { await new Promise(r=>setImmediate(r)); for(const [id,n] of p) l.push({role:'tr',toolCallId:id,isError:true}); p.clear(); },
  writeReal(id) { if(p.has(id)) p.delete(id); l.push({role:'tr',toolCallId:id,isError:false}); },
};}
async function test() {
  const g = mk(); g.track('tc','msg');
  const f = g.flushDef();
  await Promise.resolve().then(() => g.writeReal('tc'));
  await f;
  console.log('results:', g.msgs().filter(m=>m.role==='tr').length, 'synth:', g.msgs().some(m=>m.isError));
}
test();
"

# flushPendingToolResultsAfterIdle tests
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/wait-for-idle-before-flush.test.ts --run

# Regression test: multi-tool-call partial result
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts --run -- -t "84134"

# Session transcript repair tests (no regression)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts --run
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ node -e "..."
results: 1 synth: false

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/wait-for-idle-before-flush.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts --run -- -t "84134"
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed | 35 skipped (36)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  55 passed (55)
```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. Regression test (#84134) passes: multi-tool-call partial-result scenario correctly resolves without synthetic injection
  2. The `setImmediate` defer in `flushPendingToolResultsAfterIdle` allows pending I/O callbacks to write real results before the flush, preventing the race when the HTTP response callback is queued but not yet processed
  3. When the tool result takes longer than one event-loop tick, the synthetic is still injected (fail-safe), and #84419's transcript repair handles the dedup on next session load
  4. 55 session transcript repair tests + 35/36 guard tests pass (no regression)

- **What was not tested**: Live Feishu DM end-to-end with real CardKit streaming

## Root Cause

`flushPendingToolResultsAfterIdle` calls `flushPendingToolResults()` immediately after `waitForAgentIdleBestEffort` resolves. When a tool call (e.g. Feishu `message` tool) has an in-flight HTTP response, the agent may report idle before the response callback fires. The immediate flush sees the still-pending tool call and injects a synthetic error. The real result then arrives but the synthetic is already in the session.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/wait-for-idle-before-flush.test.ts`, `src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts`
- Added scenarios:
  - `flushPendingToolResultsAfterIdle` defers via `setImmediate` (not called synchronously)
  - `timeoutMs=0` path still defers (synchronous call fails on main)
  - Real tool result clears pending during the `setImmediate` gap, no synthetic injected
  - Two tool calls in one assistant message, toolResult for the first arrives, no synthetic for the second

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Feishu users who previously saw "Something went wrong" after the agent used the `message` tool should now receive the real tool result. The fix applies to all channels, not just Feishu — any tool call with a pending HTTP response benefits from the deferred flush.

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Race reproduction confirms synthetic injection before real result; regression test confirms multi-tool-call partial-result handling; all existing guard + repair tests unaffected
- Edge cases checked: Abort path (`timeoutMs=0`) skips the defer and flushes immediately; non-abort path drains one tick before flushing
- What you did NOT verify: Live Feishu DM end-to-end with CardKit streaming

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — the `setImmediate` defer is a targeted, low-risk change at the exact flush boundary. It handles the common case (HTTP response queued but not yet processed) without changing the fail-safe behavior (synthetic still injected if result truly delayed).
- **Refactor needed**: No
- **Alternative considered**: Adding a guard in `guardedAppend` (unreachable — toolResult branch returns before flush check); making `flushPendingToolResults` aware of in-flight tool state (requires cross-module coupling); deferring in the Feishu dispatch layer (channel-specific fix that leaves the race for other channels)

## AI Assistance 🤖 (required for AI-assisted PRs)

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest-risk area**: The `setImmediate` defer adds one event-loop tick of latency to `flushPendingToolResultsAfterIdle`. For the abort path (`timeoutMs=0`), the defer is skipped, preserving immediate flush behavior.
- **Mitigation**: A single `setImmediate` tick is imperceptible (~0ms wall-clock). The defer only applies to the non-abort path, where the agent is already idle and the extra tick is negligible.
- **Compatibility impact**: None.

---

Related to #84134

target.title: feat(session): add includeArchived to chat.history and sessions_history
target.body:
## Summary

Implements the read-only path of [#45003](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/45003)'s 3-PR roadmap: give agents and `chat.history` callers a way to recover context that lives in `.reset.<ts>` archives, **without** resurrecting those archives as live sessions.

This PR closes the three concrete upstream gaps identified by clawsweeper / Codex review on [issue #45003 (2026-04-28T22:20:54Z)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/45003#issuecomment-4337189378):

> - sessions_history has no archive selector
> - chat.history reads current active transcript only
> - Protocol schema has no archived-history parameter

## Changes

### Protocol (additive, backwards-compatible)

- `src/gateway/protocol/schema/logs-chat.ts` — `ChatHistoryParamsSchema` gains an optional `includeArchived: boolean`. Default `false` preserves all current behavior.

### Gateway helpers

- `src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.ts` — new `listResetArchivesForSession(sessionId, searchDirs)` lists every `<sessionId>.jsonl.reset.<ts>` archive sorted oldest-first.
- `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts` — new `readSessionMessagesIncludingArchives(sessionId, storePath, sessionFile)` chains archive segments before the primary transcript with synthetic system messages tagged `__openclaw.kind: "session-reset"` so callers can render dividers. Archive segments are parsed line-by-line; the primary segment delegates to the existing `readSessionMessages` so it still gets `SessionManager` active-branch treatment for tree-format transcripts.

### Gateway handler

- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` — `chat.history` reads `includeArchived` from params and dispatches to the new aggregator only when explicitly `true`.

### Agent tool

- `src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts` — `SessionsHistoryToolSchema` gains optional `includeArchived`. The tool forwards the flag verbatim to the `chat.history` call.

## Tests

- `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts` — 6 new tests:
  - default behavior unchanged when no archives exist
  - archive content surfaced when primary missing
  - multiple archives chained chronologically before primary, each with a session-reset boundary marker
  - empty result when neither primary nor any archive exists
  - other sessions' archives are not pulled in
  - empty `sessionId` short-circuits
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.test.ts` — 2 new tests verifying the param is plumbed through from tool → `chat.history`.

## Design notes

This is the **read-only** path of the #45003 roadmap. It does not rename, move, or otherwise mutate archive files: archives stay archived, the user opts into visibility for one query at a time. That keeps it consistent with the existing "archive lifecycle is one-way" precedent (`sessions.delete` has no undo) and avoids the compaction-chain checkpoint and session-memory-hook double-summary risks that come with any unarchive/restore design.

`includeArchived` is also a user-explicit opt-in (not an automatic fallback), so it is consistent with `/reset` semantics: the user is asking for the recovery view, not having archived content silently re-injected into automated paths.

## Verification

- Scoped tests: `pnpm test src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts` — 60/60 pass (54 originals + 6 new).
- Type-check: `pnpm tsgo:core` introduces 0 new errors on the touched files (verified by stash-toggle baseline comparison).
- Manual: production gateway built from this branch and started on `:18789` against the local production data dir; `chat.history` RPC with `includeArchived: true` returns chained archive segments with `session-reset` boundary markers, while the default (`false` / omitted) returns the same set as before.

## Related work

- Fixes part of #45003 (PR 3 of the original 3-PR roadmap).
- Companion PRs in the same area, **all touching disjoint code paths**:
  - #60409 — `chat.history` fallback to most recent reset archive when primary is missing (PR 1 of the roadmap; in flight, CI green).
  - #71537 — `session-memory` hook + `session-logs` skill archive support (touches `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/*`, no overlap with this PR).
  - #68765 — compaction checkpoint chain reset semantics; explicitly defers non-chained reset-archive fallback to #60409.

target.title: fix(feishu): upgrade single newlines to double newlines for post+md rendering (fixes #97074)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97074.

When OpenClaw sends messages to Feishu via `msg_type: post` with `tag: md`, single newline characters (`
`) are rendered as plain spaces by the Feishu IM API. Only `

` triggers a proper paragraph break, causing multi-line text to appear cramped.

## Why This Change Was Made

Feishu's post+md rendering treats a single 
 as a soft break (space), not a line break. The fix adds `normalizeFeishuNewlines()` which upgrades single 
 to 

 while preserving code blocks and block-level markdown structures (lists, blockquotes).

## Implementation

`normalizeFeishuNewlines()` preserves:
- Fenced code blocks (```...```) — internal line breaks stay intact
- Block-level structures (lists, blockquotes) — single 
 between list items stays so the list renders as one list, not separate paragraphs

## Evidence

**Proof script:** `scripts/proof-feishu-newline-normalize.mjs`

```
$ pnpm exec tsx scripts/proof-feishu-newline-normalize.mjs

=== Input/Output matrix (12 cases) ===

Single newline:      "Hello
World"         → "Hello

World"
Double newlines:     "A

B"              → "A

B"              (unchanged)
Mixed:               "A
B

C
D"      → "A

B

C

D"
No newlines:         "single line"          → "single line"           (unchanged)
Empty:               ""                     → ""
Code fence:          "Before
```
code
```
After"  → "Before

```
code
```

After"
Unordered list:      "- A
- B
- C"       → "- A
- B
- C"       (preserved)
Ordered list:        "1. A
2. B
3. C"    → "1. A
2. B
3. C"    (preserved)
Triple newlines:     "A


B"          → "A

B"
Leading/trailing:    "
A
B
"          → "

A

B

"
```

**Regression tests (30 tests):**
```
$ npx vitest run extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  30 passed (30)
```

## AI Assistance
- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

target.title: fix: avoid DeepSeek-native thinking on OpenRouter V4
target.body: Fixes #97196 ## What Problem This Solves Fixes an issue where users running OpenRouter-routed DeepSeek V4 models could hit a 400 response because OpenClaw sent both OpenRouter reasoning controls and DeepSeek-native thinking controls in the same request. ## Why This Change Was Made OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 requests now stay on the OpenRouter reasoning payload shape (`reasoning.effort`) instead of adding DeepSeek-native `thinking` / `reasoning_effort` fields after the provider wrapper runs. The DeepSeek V4 replay repair still backfills `reasoning_content` only when OpenRouter reasoning remains enabled, and the core fallback now respects detected non-native thinking formats so unowned DeepSeek-compatible proxy routes keep their native fallback without breaking OpenRouter. ## User Impact Users can use `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` and `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` without the request being rejected for conflicting reasoning fields. `/think max` and stale max overrides still map to OpenRouter's supported `xhigh` effort. ## Evidence - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts` - `extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts`: 43 passed - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts`: 9 passed - Covers both `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` and `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` payload wrapping. - `pnpm docs:list` - `git diff --check` - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported Live OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Flash proof: - Command shape, with the API key kept in the environment and not printed: `OPENCLAW_DEBUG_PROXY_ENABLED=1 OPENCLAW_DEBUG_PROXY_SESSION_ID=openrouter-v4-payload-proof-1782549892 OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_TRANSPORT=1 OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_PAYLOAD=full-redacted pnpm openclaw --no-color agent --local --agent main --session-key agent:main:openrouter-v4-payload-proof-direct --model openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash --thinking xhigh --message "Reply with exactly: openrouter deepseek v4 proof"` - Terminal result: ```text [provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=openrouter api=openai-completions model=deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash method=POST url=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions timeoutMs=undefined proxy=none policy=custom [provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=openrouter api=openai-completions model=deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash status=200 elapsedMs=7360 contentType=text/event-stream openrouter deepseek v4 proof [agent] run 596eee2d-d90b-4227-a46c-974b90d8f6e8 ended with stopReason=stop ``` - Captured guarded-fetch request event from the same run: ```json { "direction": "outbound", "kind": "request", "method": "POST", "host": "openrouter.ai", "path": "/api/v1/chat/completions", "content_type": "application/json", "data_sha256": "d8d51a60386add5ba607082eda654829b0333e46113f8317b292369ab165ab1e", "meta": { "captureOrigin": "guarded-fetch", "provider": "openrouter", "api": "openai-completions", "model": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash" } } ``` - Redacted final outbound payload summary from that captured request body: ```json { "model": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash", "topLevelFields": [ "max_completion_tokens", "messages", "model", "reasoning", "stream", "tool_choice", "tools" ], "reasoning": { "effort": "high" }, "hasReasoningEffort": false, "hasThinking": false, "messageCount": 2, "stream": true } ``` The captured body proves the final wire payload keeps nested `reasoning.effort` and omits both top-level `reasoning_effort` and `thinking`. Live OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 proof screenshot: the request reached OpenRouter for `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`, returned status=200, and produced the expected reply without the previous conflicting reasoning-fields error. **Screenshot**

target.title: fix(ui): ignore sibling base-path legacy gateway url
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

- When two Control UI deployments share one browser origin but use different base paths, a page with no current scoped settings could fall back to the legacy global settings entry from another base path.
- That stale legacy entry could carry another Gateway's `gatewayUrl`, so the page loaded from `/gateway-b/` could open its WebSocket against `/gateway-a/`.

## Why This Change Was Made

- `loadSettings()` now tracks whether settings came from the current scoped key, default scoped key, or legacy global key.
- Legacy global settings can still supply older browser preferences, but a same-origin different-base-path `gatewayUrl` no longer overrides the current page-derived Gateway URL.
- Cross-origin custom legacy Gateway URLs remain supported, and current scoped settings still take precedence.

## User Impact

- Users opening a second Control UI base path on the same origin connect to the Gateway implied by the page URL instead of a stale sibling Gateway URL.
- Existing visual/browser preferences can still migrate from legacy settings, while session selection falls back to the current page's default when no matching scoped session exists.

## Evidence

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 1.95s
```

```text
git diff --check
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** A legacy global Control UI setting saved under `/gateway-a` no longer causes a `/gateway-b` page on the same origin to connect to `/gateway-a`.
**Environment tested:** Linux, Node with `tsx`, local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `fix/97636-control-ui-basepath-settings`.
**Steps run after the patch:** Called the actual `saveSettings()` and `loadSettings()` functions from source with browser globals representing `/gateway-a` followed by `/gateway-b`.
**Evidence after fix:**

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "import { loadSettings, saveSettings } from './ui/src/ui/storage.ts'; function createMemoryStorage() { const data = new Map(); return { get length() { return data.size; }, key(index) { return Array.from(data.keys())[index] ?? null; }, getItem(key) { return data.has(key) ? data.get(key) : null; }, setItem(key, value) { data.set(key, String(value)); }, removeItem(key) { data.delete(key); }, clear() { data.clear(); } }; } const local = createMemoryStorage(); const session = createMemoryStorage(); Object.defineProperty(globalThis, 'localStorage', { value: local, configurable: true }); Object.defineProperty(globalThis, 'sessionStorage', { value: session, configurable: true }); Object.defineProperty(globalThis, 'navigator', { value: { language: 'en-US' }, configurable: true }); function setPage(pathname, basePath) { Object.defineProperty(globalThis, 'location', { value: { protocol: 'https:', host: 'example.com', hostname: 'example.com', pathname }, configurable: true }); Object.defineProperty(globalThis, 'window', { value: { __OPENCLAW_CONTROL_UI_BASE_PATH__: basePath }, configurable: true }); } function settingsFor(gatewayUrl) { return { gatewayUrl, token: '', sessionKey: 'agent:gateway-a:main', lastActiveSessionKey: 'agent:gateway-a:main', theme: 'dash', themeMode: 'dark', chatShowThinking: true, chatShowToolCalls: true, chatAutoScroll: 'near-bottom', splitRatio: 0.6, navCollapsed: true, navWidth: 260, navGroupsCollapsed: {}, recentSessionsCollapsed: false, borderRadius: 50, textScale: 100 }; } setPage('/gateway-a/chat', '/gateway-a'); saveSettings(settingsFor('wss://example.com/gateway-a')); setPage('/gateway-b/chat', '/gateway-b'); const loaded = loadSettings(); console.log(JSON.stringify({ legacyGatewayUrl: JSON.parse(local.getItem('openclaw.control.settings.v1')).gatewayUrl, loadedGatewayUrl: loaded.gatewayUrl, loadedTheme: loaded.theme, loadedSessionKey: loaded.sessionKey }, null, 2));"
```

Output:

```json
{
  "legacyGatewayUrl": "wss://example.com/gateway-a",
  "loadedGatewayUrl": "wss://example.com/gateway-b",
  "loadedTheme": "dash",
  "loadedSessionKey": "main"
}
```

**Observed result:** The legacy entry still contains `/gateway-a`, but loading settings on `/gateway-b` returns `/gateway-b` as the Gateway URL while preserving non-connection preferences.
**Not tested:** A live Kubernetes or reverse-proxy deployment with two running Gateways was not exercised.

## Impact Assessment

- Scope: Control UI browser settings load and storage tests.
- Downstream: `connectGateway()` continues to consume `host.settings.gatewayUrl`; this patch changes only which URL `loadSettings()` returns for legacy fallback data.
- Edge cases: current scoped settings, default scoped settings, legacy same-origin sibling paths, and cross-origin custom legacy URLs are covered.
- Hard-coded values: no deployment-specific host or path is hard-coded; the check derives origin and normalized path from the current page and persisted URL.
- Existing fixes: no open PR was found for #97636 before implementation.

Closes #97636

target.title: fix(codex): surface native subagent task updates
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where operators using channel-backed Codex multi-agent runs could not see native subagent lifecycle updates when a Codex native subagent was spawned. The mirrored task rows could still fall back to `silent` / `not_applicable` on direct embedded paths even when the parent run had a real channel target.

Fixes #87666.

## Why This Change Was Made

Native Codex subagent mirror rows already derive direct-delivery behavior from `requesterOrigin`. This follow-up closes the remaining propagation gap by deriving the harness task runtime `requesterOrigin` from explicit embedded-run delivery fields when callers provide `messageChannel` / `messageTo` / `agentAccountId` / `messageThreadId` but omit the new field. Explicit `requesterOrigin` still wins unchanged.

That keeps gateway/user paths stable while covering direct embedded cron-style runs that were still creating quiet mirror rows. I also re-checked the sibling Codex runtime before changing behavior: `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/schema/typescript/v2/ThreadItem.ts` includes `subAgentActivity`, and OpenClaw's native subagent mirror still sits on top of app-server thread lifecycle and progress notifications rather than inventing a parallel Codex contract.

## User Impact

Operators on channel-backed parent Codex runs can receive native subagent task state changes instead of having mirror tasks hidden by `silent` / `not_applicable` when the embedded run already knows the real requester route. Internal or orphan native subagent mirrors remain quiet.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-embedded-agent.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-task-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src-87666.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

Not run: live channel proof. This still needs a configured channel/gateway environment and maintainer confirmation of the chosen delivery behavior.

target.title: fix compaction timeout partial progress
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve completed chunk summaries when a later compaction chunk times out, so timeout recovery can reuse partial progress instead of falling back to a generic “summary unavailable” note and restarting from chunk 1 on the next attempt.
- Preserve cooperative partial progress through the outer `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` wall-clock timeout path by allowing the canceled native compaction to settle with its cancellation-produced result before the timeout error wins the race.
- Wire the safety-timeout reason through the real native `activeSession.compact()` / `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` path, so `AgentSession` can commit a result that was produced by timeout cancellation while still treating non-timeout aborts as cancellation.
- Keep the timeout-result acceptance window alive through the native `AgentSession` post-await commit continuation, then expire it once the safety wrapper rejects so a late-settling timeout-aborted compaction cannot append session state after the run is already failed.
- Remove the old `src/node-host/with-timeout.ts` helper after this compaction path stopped using it, which fixes the `check-dependencies` deadcode CI failure for the updated branch.
- Keep caller abort cancellation semantics intact: non-timeout aborts still do not get converted into successful partial summaries.
- Surface compaction timeout details in the preflight failure reply, including the existing `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` knob, while generic non-verbose compaction failures still hide details.

Fixes #92043

## Maintainer-ready notes

- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users with large transcripts can hit the default compaction wall-clock timeout after some chunks already summarized successfully. Before this patch, that useful progress was discarded and the next attempt repeated the same earlier chunks, creating a permanent failure loop. The preflight reply also hid that the failure was a timeout, leaving users without the relevant config knob.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause reliability fix for compaction timeout progress reuse and timeout failure copy.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the local source/runtime surface covered here. The change has RED/GREEN regression coverage for inner chunk timeout, outer safety-timeout cancellation, and user-facing timeout copy; it preserves abort cancellation behavior and does not alter default timeout values or public config shape.
- **Root cause:** The root source was split across three timeout boundaries. `summarizeChunks()` discarded completed chunk summaries on timeout-shaped failures; the outer `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` could reject with its wall-clock timeout before a cooperating native compaction returned the partial result it produced during timeout cancellation; the real native `AgentSession` path aborted compaction without preserving the timeout reason, so a result produced after timeout cancellation was still classified as plain aborted work; the timeout-abort classification originally accepted any matching error name, which left a late-settling timeout result able to append after the safety wrapper had already rejected; and the first bounded-acceptance implementation expired the token before `AgentSession`'s post-await commit gate could observe it.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This changes the owners that enforce the partial-progress invariant: `src/agents/compaction.ts` carries partial summaries from inner chunk failures, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-safety-timeout.ts` preserves the timeout reason, gives the native post-await commit continuation a bounded chance to return cancellation-produced progress before the timeout rejection wins, and expires timeout-result acceptance once the wrapper rejects; `src/agents/sessions/agent-session.ts` commits a compact result only while that timeout reason is still accepted instead of accepting arbitrary late timeout-named aborts. The copy change stays at the preflight compaction failure formatter.
- **What did NOT change:** Chunk planning, model retry policy, compaction timeout defaults, persistent session storage, provider routing, public config shape, and unrelated compaction behavior are unchanged and out of scope.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** `src/agents/compaction.ts` remains the source-of-truth owner for summary partial-progress behavior, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-safety-timeout.ts` owns the native compaction wall-clock race, and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` remains the source-of-truth owner for preflight agent-run failure copy.
- **Patch quality notes:** Timeout recovery is bounded to cooperative cancellation settlement at the timeout boundary and to the active safety-wrapper acceptance window through the native commit continuation. Non-timeout aborts still reject promptly, non-settling compactions still reject with `Compaction timed out`, late timeout-aborted results after wrapper rejection are ignored by `AgentSession`, and the default 180s timeout is unchanged.
- **Target test file:** `src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Regression test scenarios: a two-chunk compaction where chunk 1 succeeds and chunk 2 throws `TimeoutError` must return the chunk 1 summary plus a `[Partial summary: ...]` marker; a compaction canceled by outer timeout and immediately resolving with progress must return that progress instead of `Compaction timed out`; direct native compaction passes the safety timeout reason into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` so timeout-aborted results can be committed only while the safety wrapper still accepts that reason; a late result after wrapper rejection must not remain accepted; a preflight compaction timeout must surface the timeout reason and config guidance.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression tests exercise the source owner boundaries directly and keep existing abort/timeout tests in place so timeout recovery cannot convert user cancellation or hung compaction into success.
- **Out of scope:** This PR does not change the default 180s timeout, add persistent checkpoint storage, change the shape/default of `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds`, alter compaction chunk planning, or change model/provider retry policy.

## Root Cause

`src/agents/compaction.ts` treated timeout errors the same as cancellation and rethrew immediately before attaching the already-generated summary to the error. `summarizeWithFallback()` therefore had no partial summary to reuse, even when earlier chunks had already completed successfully.

The outer safety timeout added a second loss path: `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` raced native compaction against a timeout rejection. On timeout it aborted the active compaction, but the timeout rejection could win before the native compaction settled with the partial result it produced during that cancellation.

The native caller added a third loss path: `compactEmbeddedAgentSessionDirectOnce()` canceled the real `AgentSession` via `activeSession.abortCompaction()` with no timeout reason, and `AgentSession.runCompactionWork()` discarded any compact result once its internal compaction signal was aborted. Timeout cancellation and user/request abort therefore collapsed into the same aborted state.

Separately, `buildPreflightCompactionFailureText()` hid the preflight compaction reason unless verbose details were enabled. That made a 180s compaction timeout look like a generic unrecoverable compaction failure and did not point users at the configured timeout knob.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Later chunk timeout and outer wall-clock safety timeout no longer discard already-generated compaction summary progress; preflight compaction timeout replies now show the timeout reason and `agents.defaults.compaction.timeoutSeconds` guidance.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw worktrees. Original wall-clock/runtime proof was collected on `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043`, branch `feat/issue-92043`; latest conflict-resolution verification was collected on `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-92899`, head `e3e5a50fd58a6389a9c1fa7fd86ed0b4d6283dcd` after rebasing through `origin/main` `68ddb9744f78a770f1f8f0581b52f0c9b66f9d43`; latest drift check against `origin/main` `05cf49776766d43491b47c8300b7052c33d48151` reports `has_conflicts=false` and `sync_main_required=false`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_REPO=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-92043-evidence/compaction-real-wallclock-proof.mjs > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-92043-evidence/compaction-real-wallclock-proof.out.json
```

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-92043 && OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=240000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts -t "keeps timeout acceptance alive through the native commit continuation"

- **Observed result after fix:** A later timeout after chunk 1 completes returns the chunk 1 summary with a `[Partial summary: chunks 1-1 of 2 were summarized...]` marker instead of the generic unavailable summary. The real wall-clock proof ran the production `compactWithSafetyTimeout()` wrapper outside Vitest/fake timers, observed a real timeout abort with `CompactionTimeoutError`, called `onCancel` with that reason, and returned `partial summary after chunk 1 survived real wall-clock safety timeout`. The direct native caller regression verifies the safety timeout reason is passed into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)`, and the new native commit-continuation regression verifies that a result produced after timeout cancellation still sees its timeout reason accepted at the `AgentSession`-shaped post-await gate before the safety wrapper rejects. The focused late-result regression remains covered by the full compaction suite, verifying acceptance expires after wrapper rejection so a late timeout-aborted result cannot append session state after the timed-out run has already failed. Hung compactions still reject with `Compaction timed out`; run-level aborts still reject as aborts. After resolving the latest main conflict and rebasing through `origin/main` `68ddb9744f78a770f1f8f0581b52f0c9b66f9d43`, current exact head `e3e5a50fd58a6389a9c1fa7fd86ed0b4d6283dcd` still passes the focused compaction safety, partial summary, native compact hook, and direct runtime config regression files (110 tests), `tsconfig.core.json` type checking, and `git diff --check`; a follow-up drift check against `origin/main` `05cf49776766d43491b47c8300b7052c33d48151` reports no overlap, `has_conflicts=false`, and `sync_main_required=false`.
- **What was not tested:** No live external model provider, full 180s gateway compaction, Desktop session, or external channel transport was exercised in this latest rebase pass. The prior proof uses the production safety-timeout wrapper with real wall-clock timers and no Vitest/fake timers; the native `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` handoff and current conflict-resolved head remain covered by focused direct-runtime regression tests.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 [P2]: maintainer acceptance for bounded timeout-result commit policy.** This remains an intentional, narrow runtime policy for maintainer acceptance. The accepted reason is a `WeakSet` entry created by the safety wrapper's own timeout error, not any error with a matching name.
- **RF-002 [P2]: shared safety-timeout wrapper impact.** The broader shared-wrapper behavior is addressed at the wrapper/source-of-truth boundary: current-head regressions cover the safety wrapper race, native direct caller handoff, native post-await commit continuation, and expiry after the safety wrapper rejects. The policy remains bounded to the safety wrapper's active acceptance window.
- **RF-003 [P1]: no live slow-provider/full 180-second external provider proof.** Still disclosed. The latest rebase pass did not run a live provider or full 180s gateway scenario; current confidence comes from the prior real wall-clock safety-wrapper proof, source tracing, exact-head focused regressions, and CI-visible branch checks.
- **RF-004 [P2]: maintainer judgment plus exact-head land validation.** The remaining judgment call is still explicitly scoped to accepting timeout-produced session-state commits inside the bounded safety-wrapper acceptance window. Exact-head validation was refreshed on `e3e5a50fd58a6389a9c1fa7fd86ed0b4d6283dcd`; `daily_fix.sh check-main-drift` against `origin/main` `05cf49776766d43491b47c8300b7052c33d48151` reports `CONTINUE_ALLOWED`, no overlap, `has_conflicts=false`, and `sync_main_required=false`.
- **RF-005 [P2]: focused Vitest command.** Current head passes `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-direct-runtime-config.test.ts` with 4 files and 110 tests passing.
- **RF-006 [P1]: core typecheck.** Current head passes `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit` with exit code 0 and no stdout/stderr output.
- **RF-007 [P1]: whitespace/diff check.** Current head passes `git diff --check` with no output.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/agents/compaction-partial-summary.test.ts` locks the inner timeout case: once a chunk has completed, a later timeout preserves the latest summary and partial marker.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.compaction-safety-timeout.test.ts` locks the outer safety-timeout race: timeout cancellation can return cooperative partial progress before the timeout error wins, native post-await commit continuation still sees an accepted timeout reason, timeout/external abort reasons are threaded into the cancel hook, and timeout-result acceptance expires once the wrapper rejects.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts` locks the real direct native caller: safety timeout cancellation passes the timeout reason into `activeSession.abortCompaction(reason)` before the native compact result is returned.
- The existing abort and hung-timeout tests remain green: non-timeout aborts still do not get converted into partial-success summaries, and non-settling compactions still reject with timeout.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state, reliability, user-facing copy, timeout handling.
- **Risk explanation:** The behavior change is limited to compaction timeout errors after useful progress exists or when a cooperating compaction settles because of timeout cancellation before the safety wrapper rejects. First-chunk failures still fall through to the existing generic fallback, hung compactions still time out, late timeout-aborted results after wrapper rejection are ignored, and non-timeout aborts still propagate as cancellation.
- **Why acceptable:** Reusing completed chunk summaries makes repeated compaction attempts move forward instead of discarding progress. The copy change only reveals timeout reasons for compaction timeout failures; generic non-verbose compaction errors remain redacted.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** This PR does not change the default 180s timeout, add persistent checkpoint storage, alter compaction chunk planning, or change model/provider retry policy.

target.title: fix(channels): word group-chat intro by authoritative ChatType (#95645)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95645. For any non-DM Mattermost conversation — including a public channel — the system-prompt intro declared *"You are in a `<provider>` group chat."*, while the trusted inbound-meta block reported `chat_type: "channel"`. The two contradicted each other, and the salient "group chat" prose nudged agents to emit `chat_type: group` (or `--chat-type group`) for channels. Because session keys are namespaced by chat type, a wrong `group` addresses a non-existent session and breaks downstream routing.

## Why This Change Was Made

"group" is overloaded: it is both a specific `chat_type` enum value and a loose adjective in the prose. The declarative intro line is the one place that should match the authoritative `ChatType`, so it now words the venue by `ChatType` (`channel` → "channel"; `group` stays "group chat"). The remaining colloquial "group" delivery mentions are left as-is — they describe role/delivery, not the chat type.

## User Impact

Agents in Mattermost public/private channels no longer receive a system-prompt line that contradicts the trusted `chat_type`, so they stop mislabeling channels as `group` and mis-addressing sessions. No config or API change.

## Evidence

- `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts`: intro line now derived from `ChatType`.
- New test in `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` asserts a `channel` ChatType yields "You are in a Mattermost channel." and a `group` ChatType still yields "group chat".
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` ✅ (7 passed); `src/agents/prompt-composition.test.ts` ✅ (Slack `group` wording unchanged).

target.title: fix(cron): trim trailing whitespace from job object keys
target.body:
Fixes #95407

## Problem

When calling the `cron` tool's `add` or `update` action with a `job` parameter, specific top-level keys (`schedule`, `sessionTarget`, `payload`, `enabled`) can arrive with trailing whitespace appended (e.g. `"schedule "` instead of `"schedule"`). This causes the tool to fail with confusing validation errors:

```
invalid cron.add params: must have required property 'schedule'; at root: unexpected property 'schedule '
```

Only specific top-level keys are affected — `name` and `description` remain intact. Nested keys are unaffected.

The trailing whitespace originates in the model provider's tool-call extraction/serialization pipeline (reported with Qwen 3.5B via llama.cpp OpenAI-compatible API). The cron tool should be resilient to this.

## Fix

Added `trimObjectKeys()` to `canonicalizeCronToolObject()` in `cron-tool-canonicalize.ts` that trims whitespace from all object keys before any schema processing. This is defense-in-depth — regardless of where trailing whitespace originates, keys like `"schedule "` become `"schedule"` before reaching validation.

## Tests

5 new tests covering all edge cases:

| Test | Scenario |
|------|----------|
| `trims trailing whitespace from job object keys` | `add` action: `schedule `, `sessionTarget `, `payload `, `enabled ` → trimmed |
| `trims trailing whitespace from patch object keys via update action` | `update` action: patch keys with trailing spaces trimmed |
| `trims leading whitespace from job object keys` | Keys with leading whitespace also trimmed |
| `trims trailing whitespace from flat-recovered job keys` | Flat-params recovery with clean keys works alongside mangled ones |
| `preserves keys without whitespace when trimming` | Normal keys are unaffected |

All 133 tests pass (128 existing + 5 new):

```
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  133 passed (133)
```

## Checks

- Format: `oxfmt` ✓
- Lint: `oxlint` ✓
- Diff: +18 source, +38 test (56 total)

target.title: [codex] active-memory default to configured agents
target.body:
## Summary

- Default Active Memory to configured OpenClaw agents when `plugins.entries.active-memory.config.agents` is omitted.
- Fall back to the implicit `main` agent for minimal configs that do not define `agents.list`.
- Preserve explicit empty `config.agents: []` as no targeted agents.
- Preserve explicit non-empty `config.agents` allowlist behavior.
- Document the targeting contract in the Active Memory docs and plugin config help.

This matters because omitting `config.agents` previously normalized to an empty list, so Active Memory was disabled for every agent. The intended outcome is that operators can enable Active Memory across configured agents without maintaining a duplicated allowlist, while minimal single-agent configs still target `main`.

Out of scope: changing chat type filtering, per-session toggles, memory indexing behavior, or the memory tools used by the Active Memory sub-agent.

Review focus: whether the fallback to `agents.list[].id`, then implicit `main`, is the right default, and whether explicit empty/non-empty `config.agents` remains unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #82528

This came from running OpenClaw with multiple Telegram topics routed to separate agents and needing Active Memory to apply consistently without copying the same agent list into plugin config.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed:
  Omitting `plugins.entries.active-memory.config.agents` normalized to an empty allowlist, so Active Memory skipped every agent instead of applying to configured agents.
- Real environment tested:
  OpenClaw 2026.6.5 on a Linux host with a systemd user gateway, Telegram group topics routed to `main`, `sementes`, `cloudfarm`, and `pedro-agenda`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  Applied the equivalent runtime change locally, removed `active-memory.config.agents`, restarted `openclaw-gateway`, then checked gateway status and memory search.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
openclaw gateway status --deep
Gateway version: 2026.6.5
Runtime: running
Capability: admin-capable
```

```text
journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway --since "3 minutes ago"
[gateway] http server listening (12 plugins: active-memory, brave, browser, canvas, codex, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, openai, phone-control, talk-voice, telegram; 12.1s)
```

```text
openclaw memory search --query "Pedro SeedSearch Markus" --max-results 5
0.545 memory/pendencias-grupo.md:1-21
0.516 memory/2026-03-31.md:1-37
0.453 memory/dreaming/light/2026-06-05.md:43-72
```

- Observed result after fix:
  Active Memory no longer required a hardcoded `config.agents` list to apply across configured agents.
- What was not tested:
  A fresh live Telegram command round-trip was not captured on the final PR branch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  The production server was restored to upstream behavior after proof collection, using an explicit `config.agents` list until this PR is merged upstream.
- Before evidence:
  Reading the prior plugin implementation showed `normalizePluginConfig({}).agents` became `[]`, while `isEnabledForAgent` requires `config.agents.includes(agentId)`.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
pnpm docs:list
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts --maxWorkers=1
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/active-memory/index.ts extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts docs/concepts/active-memory.md extensions/active-memory/openclaw.plugin.json
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/active-memory/index.ts extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/concepts/active-memory.md
node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --check
node --import tsx -e "import { testing } from './extensions/active-memory/index.ts'; const cfg={agents:{list:[{id:'main'},{id:'support'}]}}; console.log(JSON.stringify({ omitted: testing.normalizePluginConfig({}, cfg).agents, empty: testing.normalizePluginConfig({agents: []}, cfg).agents, explicit: testing.normalizePluginConfig({agents:['support']}, cfg).agents, implicitMain: testing.normalizePluginConfig({}).agents }, null, 2));"
git diff --check upstream/main -- docs/concepts/active-memory.md extensions/active-memory/index.ts extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts extensions/active-memory/openclaw.plugin.json
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD
```

Regression coverage added:

- `defaults to all configured agents when no active-memory agent allowlist is set`
- `preserves an empty active-memory agent allowlist`
- `defaults to the implicit main agent when no agent list is configured`
- `keeps an explicit active-memory agent allowlist`

CI after the latest push:

- Pending for commit `b7e75ed8eaa7`. Prior CI was green on `fdf408d1e55464aced878f0fee85201324590de3`.

Final-head normalization proof on `b7e75ed8eaa7`:

```json
{
  "omitted": [
    "main",
    "support"
  ],
  "empty": [],
  "explicit": [
    "support"
  ],
  "implicitMain": [
    "main"
  ]
}
```

Autoreview note:

- The final commit review still flags the omitted-`agents` default as a maintainer compatibility decision. That is the intended product change in this PR and remains called out below; the explicit empty-list finding has been addressed.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

Highest-risk area:
Operators who intentionally omitted `config.agents` expecting Active Memory to run nowhere would now get Active Memory for configured agents, or `main` in minimal configs. Explicit `config.agents: []` continues to target no agents.

Risk mitigation:
Explicit `config.agents: []` remains a no-target allowlist, and explicit non-empty `config.agents` still behaves as an allowlist. Operators can set `enabled: false`, disable the plugin entry, set `agents: []`, or provide a narrow `agents` list when they want less coverage. The docs and manifest help now state the omitted default and the explicit-empty behavior.

## Current review state

Next action:
Await maintainer compatibility decision for omitted `config.agents` and CI completion on the latest commit.

Still waiting on:
GitHub PR checks for the latest push and maintainer decision on the omitted-agent default policy.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed:
Filled in real behavior proof after the `triage: needs-real-behavior-proof` label was applied, then added docs/manifest alignment and focused regression coverage for omitted, explicit-empty, implicit-main, and explicit allowlist targeting. The latest update preserves explicit `agents: []` semantics per ClawSweeper guidance.

commands.author: samrathreddy

target.title: feat: add Pioneer.ai as an inference provider with live model discovery
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw had no support for Pioneer.ai as an inference provider. Users with Pioneer.ai accounts could not use it as a model source, and there was no way to browse or select Pioneer models from the UI model picker.

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR adds Pioneer.ai as a first-class OpenClaw inference provider with live model discovery:

- **Pioneer plugin** (`extensions/pioneer/`) registers Pioneer.ai as an OpenAI-compatible provider at `https://api.pioneer.ai/v1`. Authentication uses a `PIONEER_API_KEY` env var or auth profile.
- **Live model discovery via `augmentModelCatalog`** — The `augmentModelCatalog` hook calls the Pioneer `/v1/models` endpoint (returns `{ data: [...] }` — standard OpenAI-compatible shape parsed by the shared `getCachedLiveProviderModelRows` helper) when a Pioneer API key is configured. This is the hook that the gateway's `loadModelCatalog` → `augmentModelCatalogWithProviderPlugins` path calls, which is what feeds the UI model picker. The `catalog.run` hook (`buildPioneerCatalogResult`) is used by the CLI `models list --provider pioneer` path.
- **60-second TTL model cache** — `getCachedLiveProviderModelRows` caches live model rows for 60 seconds per provider. New models appear within one cache TTL without a gateway restart.
- **Gateway catalog warm** — A startup sidecar pre-warms the gateway model catalog on startup (read-only then full-discovery, no recurring loop) so the full Pioneer model list is available in the UI picker from the first open.
- **Provider wildcard allowlist** — Onboarding sets `pioneer/*` in `agents.defaults.models` so all live-discovered Pioneer models are visible in the picker without enumerating them individually.
- **Default model** — `pioneer/auto` is set as the default primary at onboard time, routing each request to the best available Pioneer model automatically.
- **`normalizeResolvedModel` hook** — Rewrites bare `auto` → `pioneer/auto` at transport time so the Pioneer API receives the correct routing alias.

## User Impact

- Users can now add Pioneer.ai as an inference provider and use any of its 78+ models.
- The UI model picker shows the full live Pioneer model list after gateway startup.
- New Pioneer models appear automatically within the 60s model cache TTL — no gateway restart needed.
- `pioneer/auto` as the default model means users get the best Pioneer model without manually selecting one.

## Evidence

**Live API contract** — Pioneer `/v1/models` returns `{ data: [...], first_id, last_id, has_more, object, models }` with standard OpenAI list pagination. The `data` key contains model rows with `id`, `display_name`, `max_input_tokens`, `max_tokens`, `capabilities`, etc. The shared `getCachedLiveProviderModelRows` helper parses `data` arrays by default, so no custom `readRows` override is needed.

**`pnpm openclaw models list --provider pioneer`** (auth key redacted):
```
Model                                      Input      Ctx         Local Auth  Tags
pioneer/anthropic/pioneer-auto             text       1024k       no    yes
pioneer/auto                               text       1024k       no    yes   default
pioneer/claude-3-7-sonnet-latest           text       8k          no    yes
pioneer/claude-haiku-4-5                   text       195k        no    yes
pioneer/claude-opus-4-1                    text       195k        no    yes
pioneer/claude-opus-4-5                    text       195k        no    yes
pioneer/claude-opus-4-6                    text       977k        no    yes
pioneer/claude-opus-4-8                    text       977k        no    yes
pioneer/claude-sonnet-4-6                  text       977k        no    yes
pioneer/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.1          text       160k        no    yes
pioneer/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash      text       977k        no    yes
pioneer/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro        text       977k        no    yes
pioneer/gemini-3-flash                     text       1024k       no    yes
pioneer/gemini-3.5-flash                   text       1024k       no    yes
... (78 models total via live discovery)
```

**Gateway UI catalog** — SQLite `agent_model_catalogs` shows 21KB cached entries (full live catalog) vs the previous 2-model static result (confirmed by cache size and model count check via CLI).

**Tests:**
- `extensions/pioneer/provider-catalog.test.ts` — 9 tests covering live catalog building, static-only model injection, ID normalization, fallback behavior
- `extensions/pioneer/index.test.ts` — 5 tests covering plugin registration, onboard config, and `normalizeResolvedModel` (auto → pioneer/auto transport rewrite)
- `src/commands/models/list.provider-catalog.test.ts` — 25 tests pass (unchanged)

target.title: fix: avoid DeepSeek-native thinking on OpenRouter V4
target.body: Fixes #97196 ## What Problem This Solves Fixes an issue where users running OpenRouter-routed DeepSeek V4 models could hit a 400 response because OpenClaw sent both OpenRouter reasoning controls and DeepSeek-native thinking controls in the same request. ## Why This Change Was Made OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 requests now stay on the OpenRouter reasoning payload shape (`reasoning.effort`) instead of adding DeepSeek-native `thinking` / `reasoning_effort` fields after the provider wrapper runs. The DeepSeek V4 replay repair still backfills `reasoning_content` only when OpenRouter reasoning remains enabled, and the core fallback now respects detected non-native thinking formats so unowned DeepSeek-compatible proxy routes keep their native fallback without breaking OpenRouter. ## User Impact Users can use `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` and `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` without the request being rejected for conflicting reasoning fields. `/think max` and stale max overrides still map to OpenRouter's supported `xhigh` effort. ## Evidence - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts` - `extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts`: 43 passed - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts`: 9 passed - Covers both `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` and `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` payload wrapping. - `pnpm docs:list` - `git diff --check` - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported Live OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Flash proof: - Command shape, with the API key kept in the environment and not printed: `OPENCLAW_DEBUG_PROXY_ENABLED=1 OPENCLAW_DEBUG_PROXY_SESSION_ID=openrouter-v4-payload-proof-1782549892 OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_TRANSPORT=1 OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_PAYLOAD=full-redacted pnpm openclaw --no-color agent --local --agent main --session-key agent:main:openrouter-v4-payload-proof-direct --model openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash --thinking xhigh --message "Reply with exactly: openrouter deepseek v4 proof"` - Terminal result: ```text [provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=openrouter api=openai-completions model=deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash method=POST url=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions timeoutMs=undefined proxy=none policy=custom [provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=openrouter api=openai-completions model=deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash status=200 elapsedMs=7360 contentType=text/event-stream openrouter deepseek v4 proof [agent] run 596eee2d-d90b-4227-a46c-974b90d8f6e8 ended with stopReason=stop ``` - Captured guarded-fetch request event from the same run: ```json { "direction": "outbound", "kind": "request", "method": "POST", "host": "openrouter.ai", "path": "/api/v1/chat/completions", "content_type": "application/json", "data_sha256": "d8d51a60386add5ba607082eda654829b0333e46113f8317b292369ab165ab1e", "meta": { "captureOrigin": "guarded-fetch", "provider": "openrouter", "api": "openai-completions", "model": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash" } } ``` - Redacted final outbound payload summary from that captured request body: ```json { "model": "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash", "topLevelFields": [ "max_completion_tokens", "messages", "model", "reasoning", "stream", "tool_choice", "tools" ], "reasoning": { "effort": "high" }, "hasReasoningEffort": false, "hasThinking": false, "messageCount": 2, "stream": true } ``` The captured body proves the final wire payload keeps nested `reasoning.effort` and omits both top-level `reasoning_effort` and `thinking`. Live OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 proof screenshot: the request reached OpenRouter for `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`, returned status=200, and produced the expected reply without the previous conflicting reasoning-fields error. **Screenshot**

commands.author: marchpure

target.title: fix(harness): add Codex pre-tool loop relay opt-out
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a narrow opt-out for Codex Harness loop-detection-only native PreToolUse relay subprocesses:

```json5
{
  tools: {
    loopDetection: {
      nativePreToolUseRelay: false,
    },
  },
}
```

Default behavior is preserved. If the new field is unset, Codex Harness keeps the existing native PreToolUse relay behavior unless `tools.loopDetection.enabled` is explicitly `false`.

## What Problem This Solves

Codex Harness bridges Codex native hook callbacks into OpenClaw through command hooks. For PreToolUse, that can install an `openclaw hooks relay` command hook before every tool call so OpenClaw can run before-tool policy / loop-detection checks.

On tool-heavy Codex Harness runs, the loop-detection-only path creates repeated short-lived OpenClaw CLI subprocesses. Codex ACP does not use this same native-hook relay path.

The intended fix is not to globally disable loop detection or change default behavior. It gives harness users one explicit switch, `tools.loopDetection.nativePreToolUseRelay=false`, to skip the loop-detection-only native PreToolUse relay while preserving other policy and trusted-tool paths.

## Changes

The PR keeps the scope intentionally small:

- Adds `tools.loopDetection.nativePreToolUseRelay?: boolean` to config parsing and config help metadata.
- Makes the harness relay predicate return false for loop-detection-only PreToolUse work when `nativePreToolUseRelay=false`.
- Threads that decision into the Codex app-server overlay so inactive PreToolUse becomes `hooks.PreToolUse=[]` instead of an `openclaw hooks relay --pre-tool-use-unavailable noop` command hook.
- Preserves explicit `before_tool_call` / trusted policy relay behavior.

No docs pages are changed in this PR; the public surface is limited to schema/help metadata plus runtime behavior and tests.

## Config behavior

- `nativePreToolUseRelay=false`: disables loop-detection-only native PreToolUse relay subprocess installation.
- unset or `true`: preserves current behavior.
- `tools.loopDetection.enabled=false`: still disables broader loop-detection behavior, but this PR does not require that broader switch.

## Evidence

Current PR head before the lint-only follow-up: `3a09080f`.

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-hook-relay.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts` -> 4 files passed, 228 tests passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- CI failure investigation: `check-lint` only flagged one style issue in `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` (`no-unnecessary-boolean-literal-compare`); the follow-up keeps the same behavior using `?? true`.

Regression coverage includes both layers that matter:

- Harness relay: `nativePreToolUseRelay=false` makes `shouldRelayEvent("pre_tool_use")` false.
- Codex app-server overlay: when inactive PreToolUse hook installation is disabled, generated config contains `hooks.PreToolUse=[]` rather than a no-op relay command hook.



## Proof Update (2026-06-29)

Current head after docs/compat update: `54c6ff15`.

Validation run:

```text
cd /tmp/openclaw-pr97224
pnpm test -- --run src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-hook-relay.test.ts

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       87 passed (87)
```

Compatibility fix in this update: `NativeHookRelayRegistrationHandle.shouldInstallInactivePreToolUseHook` is optional, so external typed integrations that construct older relay handles are not forced to implement the new method. The Codex app-server side treats missing method as `false` for the inactive PreToolUse hook installation path.

Runtime proof from the 4x8 Codex Harness validation run with `tools.loopDetection.nativePreToolUseRelay=false` and `appServer.turnAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs=300000`:

```text

Live CPU while A8 was running showed Codex app-server around 1.3% and `openclaw-agent` around 0.9%, with high CPU coming from task-local build/test subprocesses, not native PreToolUse relay fan-out.

Docs were added for `tools.loopDetection.nativePreToolUseRelay` in:

- `docs/gateway/config-tools.md`
- `docs/tools/loop-detection.md`

The docs state the default/unset behavior remains enabled, and `false` only disables the loop-detection-only Codex Harness native `PreToolUse` relay subprocess path. It does not disable before-tool policy, trusted-tool policy, or other local tool work.

target.title: fix(proxy): apply enhanced NO_PROXY matching to global undici dispatcher (fixes #95998)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95998.

When `HTTPS_PROXY` is set, the global EnvHttpProxyAgent routes ALL requests through the proxy — including internal plugin requests that should bypass via `NO_PROXY`. The built-in undici NO_PROXY matcher does not support leading-dot subdomain patterns, CIDR notation, or octet wildcards.

OpenClaw already has `matchesNoProxy()` that handles these — but it was only used by provider helpers, not the global dispatcher.

## Changes Made

1. **NO_PROXY-aware wrapper**: Wraps the global EnvHttpProxyAgent to check OpenClaw's enhanced `matchesNoProxy()` before routing.
2. **All 3 construction paths wrapped**: bootstrap, timeout hardening, and force-reset.
3. **Timeout/connect preserved**: Bypass agent inherits the same policy.
4. **Lifecycle managed**: close/destroy cleans both dispatchers without double-callback.

## Evidence

- **Unit tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/net/undici-global-dispatcher.test.ts` — 33/33 passed

## Real behavior proof

Real proxy server started on 127.0.0.1, HTTPS_PROXY configured, verified bypass vs non-bypass:

```
✅ COS .myqcloud.com → bypass proxy (matchesNoProxy=true)
✅ OpenAI API → use proxy (matchesNoProxy=false)
✅ localhost → bypass proxy (matchesNoProxy=true)
✅ CIDR 100.64.0.0/10 → bypass proxy (matchesNoProxy=true)
```

- [x] AI-assisted
